title: Scaffold project and implement domain entities + SOLID source contracts
description:
## Goal
Set up the Flutter project structure and implement the foundational domain layer (entities, value objects, source contracts, repository interface, exchange capability registry) following SOLID principles and TDD.

## Effort Estimate
8 hours (one focused implementation slice).

## Implementation
1. Scaffold Flutter project
   - Create lib/ folder structure: core/, domain/, data/, presentation/, ui/.
   - Add pubspec.yaml dependencies: flutter_riverpod, riverpod, http, web_socket_channel, candlesticks, fl_chart, drift, sqlite3_flutter_libs, local_auth, flutter_secure_storage, go_router, freezed_annotation, json_annotation.
   - Add dev dependencies: build_runner, freezed, json_serializable, drift_dev, flutter_lints, mocktail.

2. Define domain value objects and entities (TDD)
   - lib/domain/entities/symbol.dart: base/quote assets, normalised id, exchange-specific rawSymbol.
   - lib/domain/entities/venue.dart: enum or sealed class for binance, bybit, okx, coinbase.
   - lib/domain/entities/ticker.dart: lastPrice, bid, ask, change24h, change24hPercent, volume, timestamp.
   - lib/domain/entities/candle.dart: OHLCV + timestamp.
   - lib/domain/entities/order_book.dart: bids/asks lists + spread.
   - lib/domain/entities/trade.dart: price, amount, side, time.
   - Use freezed/immutable value objects with equality and toString.

3. Define SOLID source contracts
   - lib/domain/sources/ticker_source.dart: Future<Result<Ticker>> fetchTicker(Symbol s).
   - lib/domain/sources/candle_source.dart: Future<Result<List<Candle>>> fetchCandles(Symbol s, Timeframe tf, {int? limit}).
   - lib/domain/sources/order_book_source.dart: Future<Result<OrderBook>> fetchOrderBook(Symbol s, {int? depth}).
   - lib/domain/sources/trade_source.dart: Future<Result<List<Trade>>> fetchTrades(Symbol s, {int? limit}).
   - lib/domain/sources/market_stream_source.dart: Stream<Result<TickerUpdate>>, Stream<Result<OrderBookUpdate>>, Stream<Result<Trade>> watch... methods.

4. Define repository interface and registry
   - lib/domain/repositories/market_data_repository.dart: abstract methods returning Result for sync operations and Result streams for live data.
   - lib/domain/registry/exchange_capability.dart: which features each venue supports (REST ticker, WS order book, etc.).

5. Implement core result type and failures
   - lib/core/result.dart: sealed Result<T> with Success and Failure; map, flatMap, fold helpers.
   - lib/core/failures.dart: NetworkFailure, ParseFailure, UnsupportedFeatureFailure, UnknownFailure.

6. Unit tests
   - test/domain/entities/symbol_test.dart
     - test_symbol_normalization_lowercase_input_converted_to_uppercase
     - test_symbol_invalid_characters_rejected
   - test/domain/entities/ticker_test.dart
     - test_ticker_spread_computed_as_ask_minus_bid
     - test_ticker_negative_price_rejected_or_documented
   - test/core/result_test.dart
     - test_result_map_transforms_success_value
     - test_result_map_preserves_failure
     - test_result_flatMap_short_circuits_on_failure
     - test_result_fold_returns_left_on_failure

## Success Criteria
- [ ] flutter pub get succeeds and all dependencies resolve.
- [ ] lib/ folder structure follows SOLID layers with no files in wrong layer.
- [ ] All domain entities are immutable freezed classes with equality.
- [ ] All source contracts return Result<T> and streams return Result<T> so failures are explicit.
- [ ] Result and Failure types compile and all 6+ unit tests pass.
- [ ] flutter analyze returns zero issues.
- [ ] flutter format --set-exit-if-changed lib test exits 0.
- [ ] No UI, REST, WebSocket, or exchange-specific code exists yet.

## Key Considerations
**Symbol normalization edge cases:**
- Inputs may be mixed case (btc/usdt) or already upper case (BTC/USDT); normalize to uppercase internally.
- Reject symbols containing whitespace or empty strings; use const assertion in tests.
- Preserve exchange-specific raw symbol (e.g. BTC-USDT for Coinbase, BTCUSDT for Binance) without leaking it into generic logic.

**Value-object equality:**
- Two Tickers with identical field values must be equal; two with different timestamps must not be equal.
- Equality on doubles must handle small precision differences via fixed-decimal types if available, otherwise document that exact-bit equality is required.

**Result type design:**
- Prefer explicit Result over exceptions for expected failures (network, parse, unsupported feature).
- UnknownFailure is for truly unexpected errors and must carry stack trace or original error.

**Freezed + code generation:**
- Run build_runner once to verify code generation works; commit generated files if team convention requires.
- If freezed causes analyzer issues, ensure build_runner output is included in analysis.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ No mutable entity classes (domain objects must be immutable).
- ❌ No throwing exceptions from source contracts (use Result).
- ❌ No UI or exchange-specific code in domain layer.
- ❌ No TODO stubs without a linked br issue number.
- ❌ No tautological tests that only verify constructors or field existence.
close_reason: Completed: scaffolded project, implemented domain entities, Result type, failures, source contracts, repository interface, and exchange capability registry. All tests, analyze, and format pass.

title: Align Portfolio screen to mockup pixel-perfectly
description: Match Portfolio screen to mockup exactly.
design:
## Goal
Update lib/ui/screens/portfolio_screen.dart and supporting widgets in lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/* to match the Portfolio/Home screen in mockups/YouTrade.dc.html pixel-perfectly per docs/mockup_audit.md and docs/design_tokens.md.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Portfolio screen renders without overflow or exceptions on iPhone 17 iOS Simulator.
- [ ] Header shows YouTrade logo (white checkmark, `#00e6d2` bg), title, subtitle, theme toggle, and Flux/Carbon direction toggle.
- [ ] Aggregated net worth eyebrow reads "Aggregated net worth · 4 venues".
- [ ] Total equity displays exactly `$746,240.00` from DeterministicMarketDataStore.portfolioNetWorth.
- [ ] 24h delta shows `+$14,820.00` and `+2.04%` from DeterministicMarketDataStore.
- [ ] Equity curve card shows 90-point deterministic curve starting at `812468.7068931296` with `#00e6d2` line, 32% area gradient, and end dot.
- [ ] Range chips are 1H/1D/1W/1M/1Y with exact active/inactive styling.
- [ ] Allocation by venue header includes asset mix label "Spot 41 · Perp 38 · Eq 12 · Fut 6 · Opt 3".
- [ ] Allocation bar shows 4 segments: Binance `#f0b90b` 41.9%, Bybit `#f7a600` 26.6%, OKX `#00e6d2` 19.7%, Coinbase `#0052ff` 11.9%.
- [ ] Exchange cards list all 4 venues with exact values and percent changes: Binance $312,480 +2.14%, Bybit $198,320 -0.86%, OKX $146,900 +1.42%, Coinbase $88,540 +0.31%.
- [ ] Each exchange card is tappable and navigates to /exchange/{venue}.
- [ ] Open positions header has "Orders →" link that navigates to /orders.
- [ ] Position tiles match mockup layout and data; tapping a tile navigates to /terminal/{symbol}.
- [ ] Widget and integration tests verify the exact net worth value and at least one tappable navigation path.
- [ ] `flutter analyze`, `flutter test`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool` all pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read docs/mockup_audit.md and docs/design_tokens.md; extract exact values for Portfolio screen.
- [ ] Update lib/presentation/theme/app_theme.dart and theme_extensions.dart if additional tokens are needed (do not duplicate existing ones).
- [ ] Modify lib/ui/screens/portfolio_screen.dart:
  - [ ] Drive net worth, 24h delta amount/percent from DeterministicMarketDataStore via provider.
  - [ ] Use 4 venue count from store-derived data.
  - [ ] Replace logo icon with white checkmark shape.
  - [ ] Wire theme/direction toggles.
  - [ ] Wire exchange card onTap to GoRouter context.push('/exchange/\${venue}').
  - [ ] Wire Orders → to context.push('/orders').
  - [ ] Wire position tile onTap to context.push('/terminal/\${symbol}').
- [ ] Modify lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/equity_curve.dart:
  - [ ] Consume deterministic equity curve from store.
  - [ ] Change range labels to 1H/1D/1W/1M/1Y and update default selection.
  - [ ] Apply exact line/area/end-dot styling.
- [ ] Modify lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/allocation_bar.dart:
  - [ ] Add 2px segment gap and 5px radius per mockup.
  - [ ] Ensure fractional shares render proportionally.
- [ ] Modify lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/exchange_card.dart:
  - [ ] Verify exact avatar, dot, kinds, value, and percent styling.
- [ ] Modify lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/position_tile.dart:
  - [ ] Verify exact LONG/SHORT badge, venue/qty, value, P&L styling.
- [ ] Add/update unit/widget tests in test/ for exact mockup values and navigation.
- [ ] Add/update integration test in integration_test/ covering Portfolio screen rendering and a tap flow.
- [ ] Run `flutter analyze`, `flutter test`, `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool`.

## Key Considerations

**Deterministic Data Source**
- Read `DeterministicMarketDataStore.portfolioNetWorth`, `portfolio24hDelta`, and `portfolio24hDeltaPct`.
- Read `_equityCurve` (90 points starting at 812468.7068931296) by adding a public getter if it does not exist.
- Do not hardcode UI text for dynamic values; only static labels like "Aggregated net worth" may be hardcoded.

**Theme/Direction Sensitivity**
- Colors `accent`, `glow`, `up`, `down`, `fg`, `bg` are direction/theme-dependent. Resolve via AppColorTheme and ColorScheme, not hardcoded single values.
- Test both Flux (default) and Carbon directions if possible without adding test-only code to production.

**Edge Cases**
- Empty/null provider state: widgets must not crash; use fallback values only if the store cannot be read.
- Zero or negative allocation shares: allocation bar falls back to equal flex so the widget remains renderable.
- Unknown symbol in position tile tap: still push /terminal/{symbol} (matches mockup navigation contract).

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No visual approximations; use exact mockup measurements, colors, typography, spacing, radii, and shadows.
- ❌ No new dependencies unless approved by the user.
- ❌ No hardcoded API keys or secrets.
- ❌ No breaking existing navigation or auth behavior.
- ❌ No skipping tests; TDD required (failing test before production code).
- ❌ No one giant PR or unrelated changes to other screens.
- ❌ No unwrap/expect equivalent in Dart; handle nullable theme tokens gracefully.
- ❌ No TODO comments without an issue number.
close_reason: Portfolio screen aligned to mockup: deterministic net worth/delta/equity curve, 4-venue exchange cards, open positions, navigation wiring, and tests all passing.

title: Implement local biometric/PIN auth guard
design:
## Goal
Implement a local biometric/PIN authentication guard that blocks access to the app's main content until the user authenticates. The guard must work offline, use the platform's biometric APIs through the local_auth plugin, and fall back to a PIN when biometrics are unavailable or fail.

## Effort Estimate
6–8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `flutter analyze` returns no errors or warnings.
- [ ] `flutter test` passes all new and existing tests.
- [ ] `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` exits cleanly.
- [ ] `LocalAuthService` interface is abstract and lives in `lib/domain/auth/`; implementation lives in `lib/data/auth/` and depends only on `local_auth`.
- [ ] `AuthNotifier` exposes a sealed/enum `AuthState` and transitions through `unknown`, `authenticated`, `unauthenticated`, and `error` states.
- [ ] `AuthGateScreen` blocks the main app content until `AuthState.authenticated` is emitted; shows a biometric button, PIN fallback input, and human-readable error messages.
- [ ] `lib/main.dart` wraps the app so the gate is the first screen shown.
- [ ] Tests verify notifier state transitions for: biometric available/success, biometric failure, biometric cancellation, PIN success, PIN failure, and sign-out.
- [ ] Widget test verifies gate shows blocked content before auth and replaces with app content after successful auth.
- [ ] No API secrets, PINs, or biometric data are persisted; only a runtime auth flag is held in notifier state.

## Implementation Checklist

### Domain layer (`lib/domain/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/domain/auth/auth_failure.dart` with failure subtypes:
  - `BiometricNotAvailableFailure`
  - `AuthCancelledFailure`
  - `AuthFailedFailure`
  - `PinNotSetFailure`
  - `PinMismatchFailure`
- [ ] Create `lib/domain/auth/local_auth_service.dart` abstract interface:
  - `Future<Result<bool>> authenticate()`
  - `Future<bool> canCheckBiometrics()`

### Data layer (`lib/data/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/data/auth/local_auth_service_impl.dart` implementing `LocalAuthService`.
- [ ] Use `local_auth` `LocalAuthentication`.
- [ ] On success return `Success(true)`.
- [ ] Map plugin exceptions/cancellations to appropriate `AuthFailure`.

### Presentation layer (`lib/presentation/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/auth/auth_state.dart` with `AuthState` enum/sealed class: `unknown`, `unauthenticated`, `authenticated`, `error(Failure)`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/auth/auth_notifier.dart` extending `StateNotifier<AuthState>`.
- [ ] Methods: `checkBiometricAvailability()`, `authenticateWithBiometrics()`, `authenticateWithPin(String pin)`, `signOut()`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/auth/auth_guard_provider.dart` exposing the notifier provider.

### UI layer (`lib/ui/auth/`)
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/auth/auth_gate_screen.dart` as full-screen gate.
- [ ] Watch auth state; show loading for `unknown`, auth controls for `unauthenticated`, app content for `authenticated`, and error message for `error`.
- [ ] Provide biometric button, PIN text field, submit button, and sign-out button when authenticated.

### Integration (`lib/main.dart`)
- [ ] Update `main.dart` to show `AuthGateScreen` as `home` or initial route, with the real app content as its child.

### Tests
- [ ] Create `test/presentation/auth/auth_notifier_test.dart` using mocktail for `LocalAuthService`.
- [ ] Create `test/ui/auth/auth_gate_screen_test.dart` using `ProviderScope` overrides.

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Biometric unavailable**
- `canCheckBiometrics()` returns false on simulators or unsupported devices.
- UI must still show PIN fallback; notifier does not emit an error just because biometrics are unavailable.

**Edge Case: Biometric cancelled by user**
- Plugin may return false or throw `PlatformException` for cancellation.
- Map to `AuthCancelledFailure` and emit `AuthState.error` without locking the user out permanently.

**Edge Case: PIN input**
- Empty PIN should be treated as mismatch, not crash.
- Hard-code a demo PIN constant (e.g., `"1234"`) for this iteration; do not store it in secure storage or persist it.
- Future iteration can replace with secure storage-backed PIN.

**Edge Case: No initial state**
- `AuthState.unknown` should show a loading indicator; do not auto-trigger biometric on first build to avoid permission dialogs during widget tests.
- Notifier constructor starts at `unknown`; caller explicitly invokes `checkBiometricAvailability()` or authentication.

**Edge Case: Sign out**
- `signOut()` returns state to `unauthenticated`; gate reappears.

**Concurrency**
- All async methods should be awaited; no parallel auth requests should corrupt state. StateNotifier's synchronous state setter naturally serializes updates.

**Security**
- Do not log the PIN.
- Do not persist auth state; it is runtime-only.
- Do not store actual PINs or secrets.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases.
- ❌ No giant exchange interface that forces every venue to implement unsupported methods.
- ❌ No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contract.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data; use WebSocket streams for active symbols.
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers.
- ❌ No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.
- ❌ No `unwrap`/`expect`-style assumptions on plugin results.
- ❌ No hardcoded secrets beyond a single demo PIN constant.
- ❌ No testing of real biometric APIs; mock `LocalAuthService` in unit/widget tests.

## Verification Commands
```bash
export PATH=\"\$PATH:/Users/deepol/development/flutter/bin\"
flutter analyze
flutter test
dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test
```
close_reason: Completed local biometric/PIN auth guard with notifier, service, and UI gate

title: Build Exchange Management screen for read-only API key input
description:
## Goal
Implement the Exchange Management screen where users can add, edit, and remove read-only exchange API keys for Binance, Bybit, OKX, and Coinbase. Keys are stored securely; only read-only public endpoints are exercised for validation.

## Implementation
1. Domain additions
   - Add  value object: venue, apiKey, secret, password, isEnabled, createdAt.
   - Add  interface: save, load, delete, validate, list enabled venues.
   - Add failure types: InvalidCredentialsFailure, SecureStorageFailure.

2. Secure storage data source
   - lib/data/datasources/local/secure_credentials_store.dart
   - Use  with key namespaced by venue id.
   - Never log values; write tests with a mock store.

3. Validation use case
   - lib/domain/usecases/validate_exchange_credentials.dart
   - Call private read-only balance endpoint for the venue using the provided credentials.
   - Return Result<bool>; do not persist balance data.

4. Presentation
   - Add  and .
   - Add  for test-connection state.

5. UI
   - lib/ui/screens/exchange_management_screen.dart: list of venues with status.
   - lib/ui/screens/exchange_detail_edit_screen.dart or modal: API key/secret inputs, enabled toggle, test connection, delete.
   - Use obscured text fields with reveal toggle.
   - Show permission hint: read-only keys recommended.

6. Tests
   - test/domain/usecases/validate_exchange_credentials_test.dart
   - test/data/datasources/local/secure_credentials_store_test.dart (mocked)
   - test/presentation/exchange_credentials_notifier_test.dart
   - widget test for ExchangeManagementScreen navigation and form.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] User can enable/disable each venue and save preference.
- [ ] API key and secret are stored in secure storage and never logged.
- [ ] Test connection calls a read-only endpoint and reports success/failure.
- [ ] Invalid/empty credentials produce a clear error without crashing.
- [ ] Delete removes keys and disables venue.
- [ ] All new code follows SOLID layers and passes flutter analyze/format/test.

## Key Considerations
**Security:**
- Keys must be trimmed but never exposed in logs or UI beyond reveal toggle.
- Warn if user enters keys with trading/withdrawal permissions (detect via exchange permissions endpoint where available).
- Secure storage migration handled by  v10 cipher defaults.

**Edge cases:**
- No secure storage available (emulator/simulator) → graceful error message.
- User cancels biometric during reveal → keep field obscured.
- Test connection fails due to network → distinguish from invalid key.
- Multiple venues saved → list loads asynchronously per venue.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ No storing keys in SharedPreferences or plain text.
- ❌ No sending keys to analytics or crash reporting.
- ❌ No writing real credentials in tests.
- ❌ No full trading permissions required or assumed.
- ❌ No TODO stubs without linked br issue.

See also:
- [docs/screens.md](../docs/screens.md) for screen flows and states.
- [docs/architecture.md](../docs/architecture.md) for overall architecture.
- [AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md) for project conventions and XP/SOLID rules.
close_reason: Completed Exchange Management screen with secure credential storage and read-only validation

title: Build Markets/Screener screen from mockups
design:
## Goal
Build the Markets/Screener screen matching the YouTrade mockup: a searchable, filterable list of markets showing symbol, name, venue, sparkline, last price, and 24h change.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `lib/ui/screens/markets_screen.dart` renders as a standalone Scaffold using the existing theme system.
- [ ] Screen shows a search bar, horizontal filter chips, header row (Symbol / Last · 24h), and a scrollable list of market tiles.
- [ ] Each market tile displays symbol, asset class tag, full name, venue, sparkline chart (fl_chart), last price, and 24h change percentage with bullish/bearish coloring.
- [ ] Tapping a filter chip visually selects it and filters the visible market list.
- [ ] Typing in the search field filters the list by symbol, name, or venue.
- [ ] `test/ui/screens/markets_screen_test.dart` verifies no overflow, renders search/filter area, and renders market list tiles.
- [ ] `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/market_screener_provider.dart` with static mock market data, search query state, and selected filter state.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/markets/filter_chips.dart` for the horizontal filter chip list.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/markets/sparkline_chart.dart` wrapping `fl_chart` LineChart for sparklines.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/markets/market_list_tile.dart` for individual market rows.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/screens/markets_screen.dart` composing the above widgets.
- [ ] Create `test/ui/screens/markets_screen_test.dart` with widget tests using `ProviderScope`.
- [ ] Run verification commands and fix issues.

## Key Considerations

**Empty / no-match states**
- When search or filter yields zero markets, show a centered "No markets found" message instead of an empty list.

**Overflow safety**
- Use `Expanded`/`Flexible` for the market list inside a `Column` so the screen does not overflow on small devices.
- Sparkline width is fixed (46px) and the price column has a fixed width (78px); remaining space flexes for name/venue.

**Mock data only**
- This screen uses static mock data; no real exchange REST/WebSocket calls are made in the UI.

**Filtering semantics**
- "All" shows every mock market.
- Other chips filter by asset class: Crypto, Forex, Equities, Commodities.
- Search matches symbol, name, or venue display name case-insensitively.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases; mock data is injected via provider.
- ❌ No TODO stubs for core UI widgets.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data in this screen (static mock data).
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers/providers.
- ❌ No deviation from mockup design system without explicit user approval.

title: Remove tautological and implementation-duplicating tests from screen/widget suite
description:
## Goal
Delete tests that provide false confidence by duplicating production logic or asserting default parameter values, reducing brittle mockup-coupled coverage.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Tests to Remove
- test/ui/screens/compare_screen_test.dart:90-103 - legend shows selected symbols and deterministic returns (computes expected text with the same sign/formatting logic as _LegendItem; tautological)
- test/ui/widgets/compare/compare_chart_test.dart:34-41 - series has 30 normalized points (asserts default periods=30 value from generateCompareSeries; tautological)

## Verification Before Deletion
For each candidate test, confirm no real bug-catching value is lost:
- Compare legend test: if _LegendItem formatting changes, the test recomputes expected with the same logic, so it cannot fail for the right reason. Delete.
- Compare chart 30 points test: if generateCompareSeries default changes, this test fails but only because it asserts the default value, not chart behavior. Delete.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Listed tests deleted
- [ ] No new tautological tests introduced
- [ ] `flutter test` still passes
- [ ] `flutter analyze` still passes
- [ ] Coverage drop accepted if it occurs (document in PR description)

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Keeping tests just in case
- ❌ Replacing deleted tests with equally meaningless assertions
- ❌ Removing tests without documenting why they provided no value

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)
- Do not delete tests that exercise user interaction or state transitions (e.g., symbol selector eviction, cancel order) even if they look mockup-heavy.
- If a deleted test was the only coverage of a file, ensure a meaningful replacement is added in youtrade-7k7.3.

1. test/ui/widgets/compare/compare_stats_table_test.dart:37-50

2. test/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/candlestick_chart_test.dart:32-39
   - Current: only checks MA7/MA25 labels exist.
   - Strengthen: assert `CustomPaint` size is non-zero, that the chart does not show `CircularProgressIndicator` when candles are non-empty, and that at least one axis price label derived from the candle data is rendered.

3. test/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/recent_trades_strip_test.dart:35-80
   - Current: asserts 300/200/100 appear somewhere in text.
   - Strengthen: assert exactly 3 (or 5 when supplied 5+) trade rows, assert first row price is 300, assert last row price is 100, assert no row shows a trade older than the 5th newest.
   - Bug it should catch: sort order inverted, limit not applied, duplicate rows.

4. test/ui/widgets/compare/compare_chart_test.dart:17-32
   - Current: only asserts container height.
   - Strengthen: assert that `LineChart` receives `LineChartBarData` spots matching the series normalized values (inspect first/last spot).
   - Bug it should catch: chart container has right height but LineChart is passed wrong data.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Every listed weak assertion replaced with an exact-value, exact-count, or exact-order check
- [ ] Each strengthened test has a comment documenting the regression it prevents
- [ ] `flutter test` passes
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes
- [ ] At least one deliberate bug injection per strengthened test causes a failure

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Replacing isNotNull with isNotEmpty (still weak)
- ❌ Adding assertions that duplicate production logic

- When checking colors, use the theme's resolved AppColorTheme value rather than hardcoding where possible, but keep the assertion exact.
- For RecentTradesStrip, use find.byType(Text) on the row widgets and verify order by widget index, not just substring presence.

design:
## Goal
Apply uppercase text transform to all eyebrow/label headers across the YouTrade Flutter UI so the rendered text matches the CSS text-transform:uppercase mockups. Flutter has no direct equivalent, so use uppercase string literals or .toUpperCase() where the text is dynamic.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Files to Modify

- lib/ui/screens/markets_screen.dart

- test/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/symbol_header_test.dart (add stat-strip label assertions)

- integration_test/auth_flow_test.dart
- integration_test/exchange_detail_flow_test.dart
- integration_test/offline_mode_test.dart
- integration_test/options_flow_test.dart

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All listed label strings render as uppercase in the affected widgets.
- [ ] Existing widget tests and new label tests pass: flutter test
- [ ] flutter analyze reports no issues.
- [ ] dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool exits cleanly.
- [ ] iOS Simulator integration tests pass: flutter test integration_test -d <device-id>
- [ ] No unrelated tests break.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Update portfolio_screen.dart labels: FLUX/CARBON TERMINAL, AGGREGATED NET WORTH, ALLOCATION BY VENUE, OPEN POSITIONS.
- [ ] Update markets_screen.dart header labels: SYMBOL, LAST · 24H.
- [ ] Update symbol_header.dart stat strip labels: 24H HIGH, 24H LOW, VOL, FUNDING.
- [ ] Update trade_ticket.dart labels: PRICE, LEVERAGE, ORDER SIZE.
- [ ] Update order_book_panel.dart header labels: PRICE, SIZE.
- [ ] Update fundamentals_card.dart tag labels, stat labels, and About section title.
- [ ] Update signal_gauge.dart section titles: OSCILLATORS, MOVING AVERAGES, PIVOT LEVELS.
- [ ] Update options_header.dart spot label: SPOT.
- [ ] Update chain_column_headers.dart labels: CALLS, STRIKE, PUTS, MARK.
- [ ] Update compare_stats_table.dart headers: SYMBOL, RETURN, VOLATILITY.
- [ ] Update existing tests to expect uppercase strings.
- [ ] Add missing widget tests for stat strip, trade ticket, order book Size, and Spot label.
- [ ] Run widget test suite, fix failures.
- [ ] Run flutter analyze and dart format.
- [ ] Boot iOS Simulator and run integration tests.

## Key Considerations

**Dynamic strings with interpolation**: 'Aggregated net worth · $venueCount venues' must uppercase the whole result; use string interpolation then .toUpperCase() or an uppercase literal with interpolated dynamic number. Keep the · separator and number as-is.

**Order book size header**: text is 'Size (\${meta.base})'. Uppercase the literal part; meta.base is already uppercase for supported symbols, but applying .toUpperCase() to the whole string is safest.

**Fundamentals stats labels**: Many labels share words (e.g., 'Market cap', '24h volume'). Uppercase all label literals in the _Stat constructors. Ensure no value text is changed.

**Avoid side effects**: Only label/header text case changes. Do not alter spacing, typography, colors, navigation, or business logic.

**TDD**: Write/update failing tests first, confirm RED, then implement uppercase, confirm GREEN, then refactor/format.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Do not change user values or data text (prices, percentages, names, about paragraphs).
- ❌ Do not use TextStyle textTransform (Flutter does not support it).
- ❌ Do not leave tests red; fix or revert immediately.
- ❌ Do not commit without running flutter analyze, flutter test, and dart format.

design:
## Goal
Move the YouTrade logo/tagline and theme/direction toggles from PortfolioScreen into ScaffoldWithNavBar so the header appears above every navigation branch, then remove the duplicate header from PortfolioScreen.

## Effort Estimate
4 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `ScaffoldWithNavBar` renders the global header on every branch (Portfolio, Markets, Trade, Options, More).
- [ ] `PortfolioScreen` no longer renders its own header; no header widgets or toggle logic remain.
- [ ] Theme and direction toggles in the global header update `ThemeSettings` and re-render the app.
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes with no warnings.
- [ ] `flutter test` passes (widget tests for the shell and PortfolioScreen updated).
- [ ] `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool` passes.
- [ ] iOS Simulator integration tests pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read `lib/ui/screens/portfolio_screen.dart:70-150` and helper classes (`_IconButton`, `_DirectionButton`, `_CheckmarkPainter`).
- [ ] Convert `lib/ui/widgets/common/scaffold_with_nav_bar.dart` from `StatelessWidget` to `ConsumerWidget`.
- [ ] Add a private `_AppHeader` widget that watches `themeSettingsProvider` and exposes the toggles.
- [ ] Insert `_AppHeader` into the shell body between `DemoModeBanner` and `Expanded(navigationShell)`; wrap header in `SafeArea(bottom: false)` and remove top view padding from the branch area to avoid double safe-area gaps.
- [ ] Move header helper widgets from `portfolio_screen.dart` to `scaffold_with_nav_bar.dart`.
- [ ] Remove `_buildHeader` and related helpers from `portfolio_screen.dart`; remove now-unused imports.
- [ ] Update `test/ui/widgets/common/scaffold_with_nav_bar_test.dart` with tests for header presence, tagline, branch visibility, and toggle behavior.
- [ ] Update `test/ui/screens/portfolio_screen_test.dart` by removing header-specific assertions and helper classes no longer part of the screen.
- [ ] Update `integration_test/app_test.dart` to assert the global header is visible on every visited tab.
- [ ] Run `flutter analyze`, `flutter test`, `dart format --set-exit-if-changed`.
- [ ] Boot iOS Simulator and run `flutter test integration_test -d <device-id>`.
- [ ] Commit changes and close the bead.

## Key Considerations

**Safe-area handling**
- The shell header must avoid the status bar, but branch screens already wrap content in `SafeArea`.
- To prevent double top padding, remove the top view padding from the `navigationShell` area using `MediaQuery` after the header's `SafeArea` consumes the inset.

**State management**
- Header reads `themeSettingsProvider` and calls `ThemeNotifier.toggleLightDark()` / `toggleVisualDirection()`.
- Shell already runs inside a `ProviderScope` via `MaterialApp.router`, so `ConsumerWidget` works without extra wiring.

**Test impact**
- Existing PortfolioScreen tests that assert `YouTrade`, `FLUX`, `CARBON`, dark-mode icon, and toggle behavior must move to the shell widget tests.
- Integration smoke test should verify the header persists across tabs.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Do not duplicate the header implementation in multiple screens.
- ❌ Do not leave header helper classes in `portfolio_screen.dart` after removal.
- ❌ Do not wrap the entire shell body in a second `SafeArea` (would double-pad `DemoModeBanner`).
- ❌ Do not leave widget or integration tests red.
close_reason: Global header added; tests and integration tests pass.

description:
## Goal
Cover edge cases that could cause production bugs but are not exercised by the current happy-path-heavy suite.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Missing Tests

### AuthNotifier (test/presentation/auth/auth_notifier_test.dart)

- test_authenticateWithPin_empty_when_no_pin_set: passing empty string when no PIN configured emits PinValidationFailure.

### AppRouter (test/presentation/routing/app_router_test.dart)
- test_unauthenticated_deep_link_to_orders_redirects: router.go('/orders') when unauthenticated -> /auth?from=/orders.
- test_authenticated_auth_route_without_from_redirects_home: authenticated user navigates to /auth with no from -> /.
- test_public_route_preserves_query_params: /markets/compare?foo=bar keeps query parameters while rendering CompareScreen.

### Screens
- SettingsScreen (test/ui/screens/settings_screen_test.dart):
  - test_biometric_available_no_pin_shows_setup: mock canCheckBiometrics=true, no PIN -> screen shows 'Set up PIN' and no 'Unlock with biometrics'.
- OptionsChainScreen (test/ui/screens/options_chain_screen_test.dart):
  - test_empty_chain_shows_no_strikes: override DeterministicMarketDataStore.btcOptionsChain to empty -> finds 'No strikes available'.
- OrdersHistoryScreen (test/ui/screens/orders_history_screen_test.dart):
  - test_empty_positions_tab_shows_empty_state: override portfolioPositions to empty -> list is empty but screen renders.
- MarketsScreen (test/ui/screens/markets_screen_test.dart):
  - test_search_unicode_special_chars_empty: enter '🚀' or '; DROP' -> finds 'No markets found', no crash.
- TradingTerminalScreen (test/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen_test.dart):
  - test_repository_error_shows_error_ui: override marketDataRepositoryProvider to return Err -> finds error widget or retry button.
- ExchangeDetailScreen (test/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen_test.dart):
  - test_unknown_exchange_id_fallback_renders: exchangeId='unknown' -> still renders Binance data and venue chips.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each missing test written and run against current code
- [ ] Each test has a meaningful assertion (exact text, exact count, exact state, or exact error type)
- [ ] `flutter test` passes after additions
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Writing tests that pass immediately without verifying the edge case
- ❌ Using production helpers to compute expected values
- ❌ Adding edge case tests with only findsOneWidget assertions

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)
- For lockout expiration, avoid real 15-minute waits; inject a controlled DateTime or use a fake clock.
- For repository errors, ensure the test exercises the actual error-handling path in the widget/provider, not just the repository.
- For unicode/special-character search, verify the app does not log or render the input unsanitized (no XSS vector).

title: Refactor production seams polluted by test-only code
description:
## Goal
Remove or rename production APIs that exist primarily to enable testing, and replace them with proper dependency-injection seams.

## Effort Estimate
8-12 hours

## Issues Found
- lib/data/repositories/market_data_repository_impl.dart: MarketDataRepositoryImpl exposes a mutable `isOnline` boolean and a `mockStore` parameter. These are direct test seams that leak into production and default to MockMarketDataStore when no real sources are configured.
- The repository should depend on a MarketDataStore interface and a connectivity-aware policy, not a mutable flag and a specifically-named mock store.

## Proposed Change
1. Replace `isOnline` mutable field with an injected `NetworkStatus` stream or bool resolved by the provider layer before constructing the repository.
2. Rename/remove `mockStore` parameter; introduce a configurable `MarketDataStore fallbackStore` that is injected differently in test vs production (deterministic demo store vs real cached store).
3. Rename `MockMarketDataStore`/`DeterministicMarketDataStore` in production fallback path to a clearly-named `OfflineMarketDataStore` or `DemoMarketDataStore` implementation of `MarketDataStore`.
4. Update all tests that currently pass `isOnline: false` or `mockStore:` to use constructor injection of the new fallback store.
5. Update `repository_provider.dart` to wire the correct fallback store based on connectivity.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Production code no longer has parameters named `mockStore` or mutable `isOnline`
- [ ] Tests still pass using constructor injection of a test `MarketDataStore`
- [ ] No behavior change in production offline/demo mode (verify with integration_test/offline_mode_test.dart)
- [ ] `flutter test` passes
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Leaving test flags in production "because tests need them"
- ❌ Making production code branch on isTestMode
- ❌ Renaming a mock to demo without changing the semantic contract

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)
- Offline/demo mode is a real production feature, not a test feature. The store that serves it should be named and structured as a production implementation.
- If this refactor grows beyond 12 hours, split into two subtasks: (A) rename mockStore and remove mutable isOnline, (B) rewire provider layer.
- Preserve the public MarketDataRepository interface so no callers change.
- Ensure the deterministic data used by integration tests remains injectable unchanged.
close_reason: Refactored production seams; tests and offline integration test pass.

title: Validate test improvements and run mutation-style regression checks
description:
## Goal
Confirm that removed RED tests, strengthened YELLOW tests, added corner cases, and refactored seams actually improve bug-detection power.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Validation Steps
1. Baseline:
   - Run `flutter test` and capture pass/fail counts.
   - Run `flutter analyze` and confirm zero issues.
   - Run `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool`.

2. Targeted regression injection (for each area below, introduce a deliberate bug, run tests, verify at least one test fails, then revert):
   - Compare stats: change bullish color to bearish color in production.
   - Recent trades: remove sorting or change take(5) to take(3).
   - Candlestick chart: make non-empty candles render CircularProgressIndicator.
   - Symbol normalization in TradingTerminalScreen: make 'BTCUSDT' not normalize to 'BTC'.
   - AppRouter external redirect: allow `//evil.com` to pass.
   - AuthNotifier lockout: remove remaining-seconds check.
   - Offline/demo mode: break fallback store wiring and verify offline integration test fails.

3. Documentation:
   - Record each injected bug and the test that caught it in the PR description.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `flutter test` passes on the branch
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes
- [ ] `dart format --set-exit-if-changed` passes
- [ ] At least 7 deliberate regressions injected and caught by tests
- [ ] No test passes after a deliberate regression in its claimed coverage area
- [ ] All injected bugs reverted before merge

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Declaring victory based on coverage percentage alone
- ❌ Skipping manual regression injection because tests "look good"
- ❌ Forgetting to revert injected bugs

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)
- Do not commit injected regressions. Use git stash or a throwaway branch.
- If a regression is NOT caught, create a follow-up task to strengthen or add the missing test before merging.
- Record the final mutation-style score: number of injected bugs caught / number injected.
close_reason: Validated test improvements with mutation-style regressions; all caught and reverted; final suite passes.

title: Add MarketDataRepositoryImpl missing corner cases
description:
## Goal
Cover error paths and edge cases in MarketDataRepositoryImpl that are currently untested.

## Missing tests
- test/data/repositories/market_data_repository_impl_test.dart
  - getTicker/getCandles/getOrderBook/getTrades when registry supports feature but venueSources map has no entry (sources == null)
  - Source returns ParseFailure with no cached data -> repository returns the ParseFailure
  - Source returns NetworkFailure with no cached data and mock fallback throws -> returns UnknownFailure('mock fallback failed')
  - Background refresh throws an exception -> must not surface to caller (current behavior)
  - watchTicker/watchOrderBook/watchTrades with no cached data and no stream source fall back to mock stream

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Add a test fixture that creates a registry supporting a venue but omits that venue from venueSources
- [ ] Add a fake source that returns Err(ParseFailure(...)) and assert the repository propagates it when cache is empty
- [ ] Add a fake source that returns Err(NetworkFailure(...)) and a mock store that throws, assert UnknownFailure message
- [ ] Add a delayed source that throws after fresh cache is returned; assert the caller still receives Success and no unhandled error
- [ ] Add watch tests with no cache and no stream source to verify mock stream fallback emits Success values
- [ ] Run flutter test

## Key Considerations / Edge Cases
- _fetchWithCache has multiple branches for sources == null, cache fresh, cache stale, source success, source failure, mock fallback
- NetworkFailure vs ParseFailure handling differs: ParseFailure is returned directly; NetworkFailure may trigger mock fallback
- Background refresh uses unawaited(_refreshInBackground); exceptions there must be swallowed
- Watch methods yield cached snapshot first, then source stream; if no source, fall back to mock stream
- Ensure mock store fallback values are deterministic for assertions

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Testing only the happy path
- ❌ Asserting only isA<Err>() without checking exact failure type/message
- ❌ Creating fakes that determine the test outcome instead of exercising repository logic
- ❌ Ignoring branches because they are "unlikely"

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All listed scenarios tested with exact expected failure types/messages
- [ ] No production branch in _fetchWithCache or watch methods uncovered
- [ ] flutter test passes
close_reason: MarketDataRepositoryImpl missing-corner-case tests already present; verified all 55 repository tests pass after format fix

title: Add auth notifier biometric and PIN edge cases
description:
## Goal
Cover AuthNotifier edge cases that currently rely on fake behavior and are not exercised.

## Missing tests
- test/presentation/auth/auth_notifier_test.dart
  - initialize when biometrics are available but authenticate() returns AuthCancelledFailure -> state should become AuthError
  - initialize when biometrics are available but authenticate() returns AuthFailedFailure -> state should become AuthError
  - authenticateWithBiometrics called when no PIN is set -> still attempts auth and sets authenticated on success
  - authenticateWithPin on first use with empty/non-digit PIN is already covered; add concurrency test (rapid calls)

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Add initialize tests where mockLocalAuth.authenticate returns Err(AuthCancelledFailure) and Err(AuthFailedFailure)
- [ ] Assert state transitions to AuthError with the exact failure type and message
- [ ] Add test for authenticateWithBiometrics when isPinSet is false (no stored PIN)
- [ ] Add rapid-tap test for authenticateWithPin that verifies deterministic final state (no duplicate authentications or crashes)
- [ ] Keep MockLocalAuthService for LocalAuthService boundary; keep FakePinAuthService for scenarios that do not depend on real PIN hashing
- [ ] Run flutter test

## Key Considerations / Edge Cases
- initialize() calls authenticateWithBiometrics() when biometrics are available; any failure there must surface as AuthError, not hang
- authenticateWithBiometrics() re-checks isPinSet before calling local auth; behavior with no PIN must be verified
- authenticateWithPin uses RegExp(r'^\d{4}$'); whitespace and non-digit rejection already covered but concurrency is not
- StateNotifier emits synchronous state changes; rapid calls should not corrupt internal _pinSet

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Testing the fake PIN service instead of notifier logic
- ❌ Asserting only state type without checking failure details
- ❌ Adding tests that pass by definition (e.g., enum case counts)
- ❌ Ignoring error branches in initialize flow

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each new test exercises production AuthNotifier code, not the fake
- [ ] Exact expected AuthState asserted
- [ ] flutter test passes
close_reason: Auth notifier biometric/PIN edge cases already covered (initialize cancellation/failure, no-PIN biometric auth, rapid PIN calls)

title: Add integration tests for offline/demo mode and auth cancellation

title: Add missing corner-case tests for data sources and repository
description:
## Goal
Add tests for edge cases in market data sources, cache, credentials, and repository that could cause crashes or data corruption.

- Binance/Bybit WebSocket clients: malformed message, stream error event, empty message, missing 'data' key, delta vs snapshot order book.
- MarketDataRepositoryImpl: unsupported venue feature returns exact UnsupportedFeatureFailure, missing registry entry, source exception path.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] No mock behavior assertions

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Happy path only
- ❌ isA<Err>() without exact failure type/message

design:
## Goal
Add tests for edge cases in market data sources, cache, credentials, and repository that could cause crashes or data corruption.

## Corner Cases to Add
- MarketCacheDataSource: empty candle/trade lists, malformed JSON in order book/trades columns, limit parameter, duplicate save updates, null returns for missing rows.
- SecureCredentialsStoreImpl: empty string stored, JSON missing fields, unknown venue id in JSON, storage read/write exceptions, concurrent read/write.
- Binance/Bybit REST clients: malformed JSON, missing required fields, retCode != 0 (Bybit API error), network timeout/exception, empty response body.
- Binance/Bybit WebSocket clients: malformed message, stream error event, empty message, missing 'data' key, delta vs snapshot order book.
- MarketDataRepositoryImpl: unsupported venue feature returns exact UnsupportedFeatureFailure, missing registry entry, source exception path.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Write failing test for each corner case (RED)
- [ ] Verify the failure reason matches the intended bug
- [ ] Implement minimal production fix if the test reveals missing handling
- [ ] Assert exact failure types/messages, not just isA<Err>()
- [ ] Run full `flutter test` after each new test group

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All P0 corner cases covered
- [ ] Tests use exact assertions for errors and outputs
- [ ] No mock behavior assertions
- [ ] `flutter test` passes

## Key Considerations
- Malformed JSON should yield ParseFailure or UnknownFailure, not unhandled exceptions.
- Bybit retCode != 0 is a real API-level error that should be treated as failure.
- Concurrent access to SecureCredentialsStore may require tests using parallel futures.
- Empty lists in cache should round-trip correctly (length 0, not null where not expected).
- Unknown venue id in stored JSON should be handled gracefully (skip or error, not crash).

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Happy path only
- ❌ isA<Err>() without exact failure type/message
- ❌ Testing fake repositories instead of production code
close_reason: All P0 corner-case tests added and production fixes applied; flutter test, flutter analyze, and dart format all pass.

title: Validate test improvements with coverage and mutation-aware review
description:
## Goal
Verify that test improvements actually catch more bugs using coverage analysis and manual mutation review.

## Validation Steps
- Run `flutter test --coverage` before and after improvements
- Identify critical paths with low coverage (auth, exchange credentials, REST parsing, cache JSON decode)
- Manually inject small bugs (e.g., flip a comparison, remove a null check, change a parse index) and confirm at least one test fails
- Target 80%+ line coverage for P0 modules and meaningful branch coverage

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Coverage report generated
- [ ] P0 modules coverage >= 80% lines
- [ ] Manual mutation review confirms tests catch injected bugs
- [ ] No surviving trivial mutants in auth/parsing paths

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Optimizing coverage percentage without improving assertions
- ❌ Adding tests that cannot fail on production changes

## Goal
Verify that test improvements actually catch more bugs using coverage analysis and manual mutation review.

## Validation Steps
- Run `flutter test --coverage` before and after improvements
- Identify critical paths with low coverage (auth, exchange credentials, REST parsing, cache JSON decode)
- Manually inject small bugs (e.g., flip a comparison, remove a null check, change a parse index) and confirm at least one test fails
- Target 80%+ line coverage for P0 modules and meaningful branch coverage

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Coverage report generated (`coverage/lcov.info`)
- [ ] P0 modules coverage >= 80% lines
- [ ] Manual mutation review confirms tests catch injected bugs for:
  - AuthNotifier PIN validation
  - SecureCredentialsStore JSON parsing
  - Binance/Bybit REST field parsing
  - MarketCacheDataSource decode paths
- [ ] No surviving trivial mutants in auth/parsing paths
- [ ] `flutter test` passes

## Key Considerations
- Coverage is a secondary metric; assertion quality matters more.
- Manual mutation review is necessary because Flutter mutation testing tooling is immature.
- Document each injected bug and which test killed it.
- If a mutant survives, create a new task to add a test that kills it rather than lowering the bar.
- Do not optimize coverage by adding weak assertions.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Optimizing coverage percentage without improving assertions
- ❌ Adding tests that cannot fail on production changes
- ❌ Declaring validation complete without evidence
close_reason: Coverage report generated, P0 modules >=80%, manual mutation review completed with all injected bugs killed by tests, full verification clean.

title: Add production auth service unit tests
description:
## Goal
Eliminate the mock-implementation mismatch in auth tests by adding real unit tests for SecurePinAuthService and LocalAuthServiceImpl.

## Why this matters
The AuthNotifier and AuthGateScreen tests use FakePinAuthService and MockLocalAuthService, so the production SecurePinAuthService hash/salt logic and LocalAuthServiceImpl platform behavior are completely untested. A bug in PIN hashing or storage would slip through.

## Tests to add
- test/data/auth/secure_pin_auth_service_test.dart
  - isPinSet returns false initially, true after setPin
  - authenticatePin returns false for wrong PIN, true for correct PIN
  - authenticatePin with no stored PIN sets the provided PIN
  - setPin rejects PIN shorter than 4 digits with PinValidationFailure
  - storage exceptions are wrapped in UnknownFailure
- test/data/auth/local_auth_service_impl_test.dart (if feasible; otherwise integration smoke)

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Create test/data/auth/secure_pin_auth_service_test.dart using a fake FlutterSecureStorage
- [ ] Implement the 5+ test scenarios above with exact assertions on returned Result/Failure types and messages
- [ ] For LocalAuthServiceImpl, attempt unit test; if platform channels make it impossible, add a documented integration smoke test and update this task with the reason
- [ ] Run flutter test and verify all auth tests still pass
- [ ] Remove or reduce reliance on FakePinAuthService only where production logic is duplicated

## Key Considerations / Edge Cases
- Empty PIN and PIN shorter than 4 digits must be rejected by setPin
- Setting the same PIN twice must authenticate correctly with the stored hash
- authenticatePin with no stored PIN must set the PIN and return true, not compare against empty
- Storage read/write exceptions must be caught and returned as UnknownFailure, not thrown
- Hash values must differ for different PINs and salts
- LocalAuthServiceImpl uses local_auth plugin; unit testing may require method-channel mocking

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Testing FakePinAuthService instead of production code
- ❌ Using the real secure storage in unit tests
- ❌ Writing tests that only assert isNotNull on non-optional types
- ❌ Leaving production auth logic uncovered because fakes exist

## Success Criteria
- [ ] SecurePinAuthService behavior covered with exact assertions
- [ ] LocalAuthServiceImpl covered or skipped with documented reason
- [ ] No remaining production auth logic exercised only by fakes
- [ ] flutter test passes
close_reason: Added production auth service unit tests and fixed SecurePinAuthService storage-read exception handling

title: Strengthen weak assertions in mock store and UI smoke tests
description:
## Goal
Replace weak greaterThan/length-only assertions with exact-value or structural assertions so the tests catch real regressions.

## Files/tests to strengthen
- test/data/datasources/mock/mock_market_data_store_test.dart
  - Deterministic ticker: assert exact seeded lastPrice, bid, ask, timestamp range
  - Candles: assert exact count, first/last OHLCV values, ordering
  - Order book: assert exact best bid/ask prices and amounts
  - Trades: assert exact price, amount, side, tradeId
- test/ui/screens/portfolio_screen_test.dart, exchange_detail_screen_test.dart, options_chain_screen_test.dart
  - Replace tester.takeException() isNull-only checks with explicit content assertions
  - Assert exact text values that derive from widget logic, not just existence

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Compute the exact deterministic values MockMarketDataStore produces for a known symbol and seed, then assert equality
- [ ] Verify candle timestamps decrease monotonically and OHLCV relations hold
- [ ] Verify order book bid/ask ordering and spread value
- [ ] Verify trade fields including side and tradeId prefix
- [ ] In UI smoke tests, assert exact rendered strings that are computed by widget code rather than only hard-coded labels
- [ ] Run flutter test after each file change

## Key Considerations / Edge Cases
- Mock store uses Random(42); values are deterministic for a given symbol and seed
- Empty candle/trade lists and empty order book should still be tested if applicable
- Timestamp assertions should account for DateTime.now() drift by using closeTo or checking UTC and monotonicity
- UI assertions must distinguish between widgets that exist and widgets that display the correct derived value

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Assertions like greaterThan(0) or isNotNull on values the compiler guarantees
- ❌ takeException() isNull as the only meaningful assertion
- ❌ Testing that a widget exists without checking the value it displays
- ❌ Replacing weak assertions with equally weak ones

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each weak assertion maps to a concrete production output value
- [ ] Tests would fail if the corresponding production value changed
- [ ] flutter test passes
close_reason: Assertions strengthened; tests pass

title: Implement Riverpod providers for market data streams
design:
## Goal
Refactor MarketDataRepositoryImpl so it can route market-data requests to real Binance/Bybit REST/WebSocket clients when configured, while preserving the mock store as an offline/demo fallback. Then add Riverpod providers that expose ticker, candle, order-book, and trade streams as AsyncValue states, plus a repository provider that wires real sources and a selected-symbol provider defaulting to BTC/USDT @ Binance.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `MarketDataRepositoryImpl` accepts `Map<Venue, VenueSources>` without breaking existing constructor callers.
- [ ] When a venue has `VenueSources`, REST and WebSocket methods use the real source; otherwise they fall back to the mock store.
- [ ] Unsupported-feature checks still return `UnsupportedFeatureFailure` before any source/mock is invoked.
- [ ] `lib/presentation/providers/repository_provider.dart` constructs the repository with real Binance/Bybit REST + WebSocket clients and a static capability registry.
- [ ] `lib/presentation/providers/selected_symbol_provider.dart` defaults to BTC/USDT on Binance.
- [ ] `lib/presentation/providers/market_data_providers.dart` exposes `tickerStreamProvider`, `candlesProvider`, `orderBookStreamProvider`, and `tradesStreamProvider` using `family` with `TradingSymbol`.
- [ ] Providers convert `Result` from the repository into `AsyncData`/`AsyncError` (throw failures so Riverpod wraps them).
- [ ] `test/presentation/providers/market_data_providers_test.dart` verifies AsyncData after `Success` and AsyncError after `Failure` for each provider.
- [ ] `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Study `MarketDataRepository`, `MarketDataRepositoryImpl`, exchange source contracts, Binance/Bybit clients, and `ExchangeCapabilityRegistry`.
- [ ] Define `VenueSources` in `lib/data/repositories/market_data_repository_impl.dart` bundling `TickerSource`, `CandleSource`, `OrderBookSource`, `TradeSource`, and `MarketStreamSource`.
- [ ] Refactor `MarketDataRepositoryImpl` constructor to accept optional `Map<Venue, VenueSources> venueSources`.
- [ ] Update each repository method to prefer real source when present and capable, else mock store, else `UnsupportedFeatureFailure`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/repository_provider.dart` with:
  - A private static registry implementation of `ExchangeCapabilityRegistry` covering Binance and Bybit with all `MarketDataFeature` values.
  - A `marketDataRepositoryProvider` `Provider<MarketDataRepository>` that instantiates `BinanceRestClient`, `BinanceWebSocketClient`, `BybitRestClient`, `BybitWebSocketClient`, and `MockMarketDataStore`, wiring them into `VenueSources` for Binance and Bybit.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/selected_symbol_provider.dart` with a `StateProvider<TradingSymbol>` defaulting to `TradingSymbol(base: 'BTC', quote: 'USDT', venue: Venue.binance, rawSymbol: 'BTCUSDT')`.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/market_data_providers.dart` with:
  - `tickerStreamProvider` (`StreamProvider.family<Ticker, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.watchTicker(...)`.
  - `candlesProvider` (`FutureProvider.family<List<Candle>, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.getCandles(..., Timeframe.h1)`.
  - `orderBookStreamProvider` (`StreamProvider.family<OrderBook, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.watchOrderBook(...)`.
  - `tradesStreamProvider` (`StreamProvider.family<List<Trade>, TradingSymbol>`) via `repository.watchTrades(...)`.
  - Map each `Result` with `fold(...)` to throw `Failure` on error so Riverpod emits `AsyncError`.
- [ ] Add `test/presentation/providers/market_data_providers_test.dart`:
  - Fake `MarketDataRepository` returning `Success` for all methods.
  - Fake `MarketDataRepository` returning `Failure` for all methods.
  - One test per provider asserting `AsyncData`/`AsyncError`.
- [ ] Run `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test`; fix issues.
- [ ] Commit all changes with the specified message.
- [ ] Close `youtrade-8s9` with the specified reason.

## Key Considerations

### Edge Case: Unsupported Venue
If the registry returns no capability for a venue, or the capability does not support the requested feature, the repository must immediately return `UnsupportedFeatureFailure` without touching real or mock sources.

### Edge Case: Source Present but Capability Offline
If `venueSources` contains a venue but the registry says the feature is unsupported, the repository returns `UnsupportedFeatureFailure` rather than using the source. Capability is the gate.

### Edge Case: Real Source Returns Failure
When a real source returns `Err(...)`, propagate that failure directly (do not fall back to mock). The caller/provider decides how to surface it.

### Edge Case: Empty `venueSources`
With `venueSources` null or empty, every supported-capability request falls back to `MockMarketDataStore`. Existing repository tests must continue to pass unchanged.

### Edge Case: Provider Disposal
`StreamProvider.family` will cancel the underlying stream when the provider is no longer listened to. No manual subscription management is required because Riverpod owns the provider lifecycle.

### Concurrency
The repository is stateless; multiple providers can call it concurrently. Real REST clients each own their own `http.Client`. WebSocket streams are created per `watch*` call.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No exchange-specific parsing or URL building in providers or UI.
- ❌ No giant exchange interface; keep source contracts small and segregated.
- ❌ No TODO stubs for repository or provider implementations.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data; use existing WebSocket streams.
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers/providers.
- ❌ No `AsyncValue` exposed as `Result`; providers unwrap `Result`.
- ❌ No direct `http` or `WebSocketChannel` usage outside the data layer.
close_reason: Completed Riverpod providers for market data with real Binance/Bybit wiring and fallback to mock store

title: Align Compare screen to mockup pixel-perfectly
description: Match Compare screen to mockup exactly and make compare button functional.
design:
## Goal
Update lib/ui/screens/compare_screen.dart and supporting widgets (lib/ui/widgets/compare/*) to match the mockup Compare screen exactly per docs/mockup_audit.md and docs/design_tokens.md.

## Effort Estimate
6 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Title reads 'Compare' in Space Grotesk 18px weight 600 letter-spacing -0.02em and count indicator reads '${selectedCount}/4 · normalized %' in JetBrains Mono 9px fg3.
- [ ] Symbol chips for BTC, ETH, SOL, AAPL, GOLD are selectable (max 4) with active style using symbol color bg/border/white text and inactive style chip bg/line/fg2 text per mockup colors.
- [ ] Normalized percentage chart renders at 220px height with 10px radius, surface bg, subtle border, grid at rgba(255,255,255,0.045), zero line, 1.8px symbol-colored lines, and 3px end dots at right edge.
- [ ] Legend shows color bar, symbol, and total return percent with up/down color; layout wraps with 14px gap and 14px vertical margin.
- [ ] 30-period stats table has 'Symbol / Return / Volatility' headers and one row per selected symbol with correct right-aligned formatting; section eyebrow reads '30-period stats'.
- [ ] Time range selector, correlation card, and ratio card from current implementation are removed; only mockup components remain.
- [ ] Trading Terminal compare button navigates to /markets/compare and route remains public.
- [ ] All compare data is deterministic via DeterministicMarketDataStore (or equivalent deterministic generator) so pixel values are reproducible.
- [ ] Widget tests verify symbol selection (add/remove/max 4), chart rendering with end dots, legend values, and stats table rows/columns.
- [ ] Integration test verifies navigation from terminal compare button to Compare screen and that the Compare screen renders without overflow or exceptions on iOS Simulator.
- [ ] flutter analyze passes with no issues, flutter test passes, and dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool exits clean.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read mockup_audit.md section 6 (Compare) and design_tokens.md runtime dark tokens.
- [ ] Refactor compare_screen.dart:
  - Remove AppBar, use body-only layout matching mockup chrome.
  - Update title/count indicator styling.
  - Remove TimeRangeSelector widget usage and import.
  - Remove _buildStatCards correlation/ratio row.
  - Keep SymbolSelector, CompareChart, legend, and CompareStatsTable.
  - Hard-code '30-period stats' eyebrow (remove dynamic pointCount reference).
- [ ] Update SymbolSelector:
  - Replace compareSymbols with BTC, ETH, SOL, AAPL, GOLD.
  - Use exact mockup colors: BTC #00e6d2, ETH #ffb020, SOL #ff5d77, AAPL #8b9cf0, GOLD #c9a6ff.
  - Active chip: bg symbol.color, border symbol.color, text white, JetBrains Mono 11px weight 600.
  - Inactive chip: bg #10151f, border rgba(255,255,255,0.07), text rgba(255,255,255,0.55).
- [ ] Update CompareChart:
  - Use theme surface (#0e131f) and borderSubtle.
  - Set line stroke width to 1.8.
  - Add 3px end dots at the last spot of each series.
  - Grid color rgba(255,255,255,0.045).
  - Draw horizontal zero line at y=0 with rgba(255,255,255,0.5).
  - Remove FlBorderData outer border if conflicting; keep container border.
- [ ] Update legend spacing to 14px gap and 14px vertical margin; color bar 14×3 radius 2.
- [ ] Update CompareStatsTable:
  - Section eyebrow '30-period stats' JetBrains Mono 9px letter-spacing 0.1em uppercase fg3.
  - Header row bg surface, border bottom, 3-column grid, JetBrains Mono 8.5px letter-spacing 0.06em uppercase fg3.
  - Data row padding 11px 14px, border bottom, symbol in JetBrains Mono 12px weight 600 fg, return right-aligned up/down color, volatility right-aligned fg2.
- [ ] Remove stat_card.dart and time_range_selector.dart files if unused.
- [ ] Wire DeterministicMarketDataStore for compare data or adapt generateCompareSeries to use deterministic seed matching mockup data.
- [ ] Verify /markets/compare route remains public and terminal compare button uses context.go('/markets/compare').
- [ ] Write failing widget tests first for title, chips, chart, legend, stats table.
- [ ] Write/update integration test for compare button navigation and Compare screen rendering.
- [ ] Run flutter test, flutter analyze, and dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool.

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Empty selection**
- SymbolSelector enforces at least one selected symbol (cannot deselect last). Tests must verify this.

**Edge Case: Max 4 symbols**
- Selecting a 5th symbol must evict the oldest (FIFO) to keep count at 4. Tests must verify.

**Edge Case: Deterministic data**
- All generated series must be seeded so values, returns, and volatility are identical across runs and screenshots. Avoid Random seeded by wall clock.

**Edge Case: Chart with one series**
- Chart, legend, and table must still render correctly with a single selection.

**Edge Case: Public route**
- /markets/compare must not require auth. Any change must not add auth guard.

**Reference Implementation**
- See mockups/YouTrade.dc.html compare section for exact DOM/structure.
- See docs/design_tokens.md for color, type, and spacing tokens.

## Anti-patterns
- NO visual approximations; every value must match mockup_audit.md.
- NO new dependencies unless approved; fl_chart is already present.
- NO hardcoded API keys.
- NO breaking existing navigation or auth behavior; /markets and /markets/compare must remain public.
- NO skipping tests; TDD required.
- NO one giant PR.
- NO leaving unused files or imports after removal.
close_reason: Compare screen aligned to mockup: title/count, symbol chips, 220px normalized chart with end dots, legend, 30-period stats table; terminal compare button navigates to /markets/compare; widget and iOS Simulator integration tests pass; flutter test/analyze/format clean.

title: Update bottom navigation to mockup tabs (Portfolio/Markets/Trade/Options/More)
description: Update scaffold_with_nav_bar and app_router so bottom navigation matches the mockup: Portfolio, Markets, Trade, Options, More. Wire Options and More/Settings routes correctly.
design:
## Goal
Update the bottom navigation bar to match the mockup tabs exactly: Portfolio, Markets, Trade, Options, More. Update route wiring so Options defaults to /markets/options/BTC and More routes to /account (Settings). Preserve existing auth gating behavior.

## Scope
- lib/ui/widgets/common/scaffold_with_nav_bar.dart — labels, icons, active states, styling.
- lib/presentation/routing/app_router.dart — branch ordering, route paths, public-route set.
- test/presentation/routing/app_router_test.dart — update existing nav tests, add Options/More coverage, verify auth gating.
- integration_test/app_test.dart — update smoke test tab labels and add Options/More screenshots.

## Effort Estimate
4–6 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Bottom nav renders 5 labels in order: Portfolio, Markets, Trade, Options, More.
- [ ] Each nav destination uses an icon semantically matching its mockup tab (Portfolio=pie/portfolio, Markets=chart/market, Trade=trade ticket, Options=options chain, More=grid/menu).
- [ ] Active destination shows #00e6d2 accent; inactive shows rgba(255,255,255,0.34).
- [ ] Tapping Portfolio → '/', Markets → '/markets', Trade → '/trading', Options → '/markets/options/BTC', More → '/account'.
- [ ] /markets and /markets/compare remain public; all other routes including /markets/options/* and /markets/exchange/* require auth.
- [ ] Existing widget tests pass; new tests fail before implementation and pass after.
- [ ] flutter analyze, flutter test, and dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool all pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read docs/mockup_audit.md and docs/design_tokens.md for bottom-nav tokens.
- [ ] Update ScaffoldWithNavBar NavigationDestination list to Portfolio/Markets/Trade/Options/More with matching Material icons.
- [ ] Update app_router StatefulShellBranch order to match nav order and wire route paths correctly.
- [ ] Change Trading branch route path from /trading?symbol=... to /trade?symbol=... ONLY if the mockup implies it; otherwise keep /trading and change label to Trade. (Current task says label Trade; keep route /trading unless tests dictate.)
- [ ] Add Options branch route that redirects /markets/options to /markets/options/BTC or uses default parameter.
- [ ] Ensure publicRoutes set contains exactly {'/markets', '/markets/compare'}.
- [ ] Update app_router_test: existing bottom nav test labels Trading→Trade, Orders→Options, Account→More; add Options and More route assertions.
- [ ] Add widget tests for ScaffoldWithNavBar icon/label rendering using a fake StatefulNavigationShell or pump via app_router.
- [ ] Update integration_test/app_test.dart to tap Trade, Options, More and take screenshots.
- [ ] Run full verification commands and fix any analyzer/format/test failures.

## Key Considerations / Edge Cases

**Auth gating around nested markets routes:**
- Public routes are exact paths /markets and /markets/compare only.
- /markets/options/BTC, /markets/exchange/:id, and /trading must redirect unauthenticated users to /auth with correct `from` query parameter.
- Verify by running the existing public-route tests and adding /markets/options redirect test.

**StatefulShellBranch index alignment:**
- The branch index must match the NavigationBar destination index exactly, or tapping will route to the wrong screen.
- Test: after tapping each label, NavigationBar.selectedIndex equals expected index (0–4).

**Options default symbol:**
- /markets/options (no symbol) must render BTC options chain. Implement either a redirect or a default parameter.
- Test: navigating to /markets/options lands on OptionsChainScreen with symbol 'BTC'.

**Icon parity:**
- Mockup uses custom SVGs; use nearest Material icon to avoid adding flutter_svg dependency per anti-pattern. Document chosen icon mapping in a comment.

**No regression on existing public behavior:**
- Do not change /auth redirect logic for authenticated users or the deep-link fallback behavior.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No visual approximations of labels or active states.
- ❌ No new dependencies such as flutter_svg unless explicitly approved.
- ❌ No hardcoded API keys or secrets.
- ❌ No breaking existing navigation or auth behavior.
- ❌ No TODOs without issue numbers.
- ❌ No skipping tests after changes.
- ❌ No one giant commit; this task gets its own focused commit.
close_reason: Bottom navigation updated to mockup tabs; routes, widget tests, integration test, and verification all pass.

title: Run auth notifier PIN tests against production PIN behavior
description:
## Goal
Close the gap between auth_notifier tests and production PIN auth behavior.

## Files
- test/presentation/auth/auth_notifier_test.dart
- test/fakes/fake_pin_auth_service.dart
- test/fakes/racey_fake_pin_auth_service.dart
- lib/data/auth/secure_pin_auth_service.dart

## Issue
AuthNotifier PIN tests use FakePinAuthService / RaceyFakePinAuthService, which do not implement production validation (digits-only, exact 4 digits in notifier vs min 4 in service, salt/hash, UnknownFailure on storage errors). This means integration bugs between AuthNotifier and SecurePinAuthService are not caught.

## Success Criteria

title: Remove tautological SHA-256 hashing test in secure_pin_auth_service_test
description:
## Goal
Remove the test that duplicates production hashing logic and therefore provides false confidence.

## Effort Estimate
1 hour

## Test to Remove
- test/data/auth/secure_pin_auth_service_test.dart:129 - 'stores SHA-256 of salt followed by PIN'
  - The test reads the generated salt and recomputes sha256.convert(utf8.encode('$salt$pin')).toString(), which is exactly the production implementation in _hashPin.
  - This is a tautological test per SRE test-effectiveness standards.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] The listed test is deleted from test/data/auth/secure_pin_auth_service_test.dart
- [ ] Remaining property-based tests ('stores a salted hash of the PIN', 'uses salt to produce different hashes for the same PIN') still pass

## Key Considerations

- Do not replace with another test that recomputes the production hash
- Do not keep the test 'just in case'
