title: Align Exchange Detail screen to mockup pixel-perfectly
description: Match Exchange Detail to mockup exactly and make venue chips functional.
design:
## Goal
Align lib/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen.dart and its supporting widgets to the Exchange Detail section of mockups/YouTrade.dc.html and docs/mockup_audit.md, using DeterministicMarketDataStore for exact venue-specific data. Make venue chips functional. Remove sections not in the mockup.

## Effort Estimate
6 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Pixel-perfect match per mockup_audit.md §4: back link, venue chips, header with API live indicator, kinds label, Balance/24h P&L cards, Balances list.
- [ ] Tapping Binance/Bybit/OKX/Coinbase chips switches venue and loads exact data from DeterministicMarketDataStore.
- [ ] /markets/exchange/:id route renders and is reachable from Portfolio exchange cards (already wired).
- [ ] No Recent Trades or API status banner card sections remain.
- [ ] Widget tests pass and assert exact strings for Binance default and Bybit/OKX/Coinbase after taps.
- [ ] Integration test navigates from Portfolio to Exchange Detail and switches venue.
- [ ] flutter analyze, flutter test, dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool pass.

## Implementation Checklist
1. Add ExchangeBalance model and exchangeDetailFor(Venue, {required Color accent}) to lib/data/datasources/mock/deterministic_market_data_store.dart, computing asset values from deterministic candles and formatting total/pnl/pnlPercent/kinds exactly as the mockup.
2. Update lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/venue_style.dart so venueColor resolves OKX to the provided accent color; other venues use mockup hex colors.
3. Rewrite lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/venue_chip_row.dart to match mockup chip metrics (gap 6, padding 7/14, radius 7, Space Grotesk 13 weight 600, active fg/bg/dark, inactive transparent/fg2/line). Iterate only the four real venues.
4. Rewrite lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/venue_header.dart to show color dot, name, and API LIVE indicator with glow; remove allocation percent; use exact mockup spacing and typography.
5. Update lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/balance_card.dart and pnl_card.dart to match mockup card metrics and typography exactly (padding 14, radius 10, bg card, border line, label uppercase 9px letter-spacing 0.07, value Space Grotesk 24 letter-spacing -0.03 tabular, P&L percent JetBrains Mono 11).
6. Update lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/asset_balance_tile.dart to accept the new ExchangeBalance, use exact row spacing/padding/glyph styling and allocation bar (bg chip, fill venue color, height 4 radius 2) with integer share.
7. Rewrite lib/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen.dart as StatefulWidget; read exchangeId, compute snapshot from DeterministicMarketDataStore; build back link, chip row, header, cards, balances list; remove ApiStatusBanner and TradeHistoryList; wire chip onVenueSelected to setState.
8. Delete lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/api_status_banner.dart and lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/trade_history_list.dart.
9. Update test/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen_test.dart to assert Binance exact strings, tap each venue chip and assert corresponding total/pnl/assets; assert back link is present.
10. Add integration test in integration_test/exchange_detail_flow_test.dart that taps a Portfolio exchange card, verifies Exchange Detail, taps a different venue chip, and verifies values.
11. Run flutter analyze, flutter test, dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool.

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Unknown / invalid exchange id**
- _resolveVenue falls back to Venue.unknown; the screen should still render safely. Because unknown has no data, default to Binance data when the venue is unknown and add a test that invalid ids do not throw.

**Edge Case: Theme / direction**
- Colors like OKX accent and up/down depend on the current theme. Widget tests must use AppTheme.dark(AppVisualDirection.flux) to match the mockup default. Ensure venueColor receives accent from AppColorTheme.

**Edge Case: Share rounding**
- The mockup rounds shares to integer with toFixed(0) and clamps bar width 0-100. Ensure ExchangeBalance.sharePercent is an integer clamped to [0, 100].

**Edge Case: Large totals / overflow**
- Balance/P&L values use FittedBox to avoid overflow on smaller screens while keeping the mockup font size.

**Edge Case: Synchronous deterministic data**
- Deterministic data is synchronous; keep screen state local and instant. No loading spinners needed.

## Anti-patterns
- NO hardcoded API keys.
- NO visual approximations; use exact values from docs/mockup_audit.md.
- NO new dependencies; use existing fonts and Material widgets.
- NO leftover unused widgets; delete ApiStatusBanner and TradeHistoryList.
- NO breaking existing navigation or auth behavior.
- NO tests that only assert existence; assert exact expected strings after taps.

title: Build Options Chain screen from mockups
design:
## Goal
Build the Options Chain screen from the YouTrade mockups using static synthetic options data. The screen must match the design system (dark/light, Flux/Carbon themes), show an underlying symbol header, an expiry selector, column headers, and a scrollable strike grid with calls/puts displaying bid/ask mark, IV, delta, volume, and open interest. This is a mock/synthetic-only implementation; real options data feed is deferred per epic scope.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `lib/ui/screens/options_chain_screen.dart` exists as a standalone `Scaffold` using the existing `AppTheme`/`AppColorTheme` system.
- [ ] `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/` contains at least `expiry_selector.dart`, `strike_row.dart`, and `greek_badge.dart`.
- [ ] Static mock options data is provided in-screen or via a private mock helper; no real API/WebSocket calls are made from the UI.
- [ ] The screen renders the underlying symbol (BTC), spot price, expiry pills, section headers (Calls / Strike / Puts), sub-headers (IV, Δ, Mark), and at least 9 strike rows with calls and puts side-by-side.
- [ ] The ATM strike is visually highlighted and labelled, matching the mockup.
- [ ] Widget test `test/ui/screens/options_chain_screen_test.dart` passes, verifying no overflow and the presence of the expiry selector and strike rows.
- [ ] `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` all pass.
- [ ] `lib/main.dart` and global routing are not modified.
- [ ] No exchange API secrets, no TODO stubs for domain entities, no polling, and no direct exchange logic in UI.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read `/mockups/YouTrade.dc.html` Options Chain section (lines 414-447) and the theme files (`lib/presentation/theme/app_theme.dart`, `theme_extensions.dart`).
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/screens/options_chain_screen.dart` as a `ConsumerStatefulWidget` or `ConsumerWidget` Scaffold with synthetic state (selected expiry index, mock rows).
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/expiry_selector.dart` for horizontally scrollable expiry pills.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/strike_row.dart` for a single 7-column grid row (call IV, call Δ, call Mark, Strike, put Mark, put Δ, put IV).
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/greek_badge.dart` reusable small label/badge widget (used for IV/Δ labels and the OPTIONS class chip).
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/options_header.dart` (optional) encapsulating symbol + spot price header.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/options_chain/chain_column_headers.dart` (optional) encapsulating Calls/Strike/Puts and IV/Δ/Mark headers.
- [ ] Generate synthetic options data deterministically inside the screen (no external dependencies): underlying BTC ~$68k, strikes from ATM-4 steps to ATM+4 steps in $2k increments, expiries 26 JUN, 25 JUL, 29 AUG, 26 SEP.
- [ ] Add `test/ui/screens/options_chain_screen_test.dart` with pump-and-settle tests:
  - `options_chain_screen_renders_without_overflow`: wraps screen in `MaterialApp` with a dark `AppTheme.flux` theme, pumps, verifies no overflow via `takeException` and finds expiry selector / strike rows.
  - `options_chain_screen_shows_expiry_selector_and_strike_rows`: finds expiry chips and strike row text.
- [ ] Run `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` and fix issues.
- [ ] Commit with the provided message.
- [ ] Close task with `br close youtrade-i0f --reason \"Completed Options Chain screen with synthetic data and widget tests\"`.

## Key Considerations

**Synthetic Data Only**
- All option values (IV, delta, mark, volume, OI) must be hard-coded or produced by a private helper inside the screen file. No repository call, no provider, no WebSocket subscription.

**Theme Conformance**
- Use `theme.extension<AppColorTheme>()!` for bullish/bearish/accent/border colors.
- Use `theme.textTheme.labelSmall/labelMedium/labelLarge/bodySmall` for typography and add `fontFamily: 'Geist'` / `fontFamily: 'Space Grotesk'` where the mockup uses those families.
- Text styles must respect the current theme; do not hard-code dark-theme colors.

**Layout Safety**
- The strike grid is dense on a 390pt-wide phone. Use `LayoutBuilder` or `Expanded` in row columns and ensure text does not overflow. Keep `fontSize` <= 9.5 for grid numbers.
- Wrap expiry pills in `SingleChildScrollView` with `scrollDirection: Axis.horizontal`.
- Wrap the full screen in `SafeArea` + `SingleChildScrollView` to avoid bottom overflow.

**Edge Cases**
- Empty/mock row list: the screen should still render headers and an empty-state message if rows are empty (defensive).
- Long spot prices: format with grouping separators and no fractional cents for BTC.
- Theme toggles: widget test should pass in both light and dark if feasible; at minimum test the default dark theme.

**Widget Test Quality**
- Tests must assert real outcomes, not existence of the widget class itself.
- Verify the expiry selector text (e.g., '26 JUN') appears and at least one strike row text (e.g., '70,000') is present.
- Use `tester.pumpWidget(...)` with a real `MaterialApp` and the app theme.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases; architecture must go through repository/interfaces (N/A here because data is synthetic).
- ❌ No giant exchange interface forcing unsupported methods.
- ❌ No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contracts.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data; this screen is synthetic-only.
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers; use `setState` inside the screen state object for the synthetic UI state.
- ❌ No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.
- ❌ No `lib/main.dart` or global routing changes.

title: Build Trading Terminal screen from mockups
design:
## Goal
Build the Trading Terminal screen from the YouTrade mockups as a standalone Scaffold. The screen displays a symbol header, candlestick chart with MA7/MA25 labels and volume, time-frame selector, lower tab bar (Trade/Book/Info/Signals), and tab-specific panels: trade ticket, order book with depth bars, fundamentals card, and technical-signals gauge with oscillator/MA/pivot tables.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `lib/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen.dart` exists, compiles, and is a standalone `Scaffold`.
- [ ] All new widgets live under `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/`.
- [ ] Screen consumes existing providers: `tickerStreamProvider`, `candlesProvider`, `orderBookStreamProvider`, `tradesStreamProvider`.
- [ ] Chart renders via the `candlesticks` package, maps domain `Candle` entities to package `Candle`s, shows volume bars, and displays MA7/MA25 labels.
- [ ] Lower tabs (Trade/Book/Info/Signals) switch panels without errors.
- [ ] Trade ticket includes buy/sell toggle, order-type chips, leverage slider, size-percent buttons, price display, and a submit CTA.
- [ ] Order-book panel renders asks and bids with depth-bar backgrounds and a spread line.
- [ ] Widget test `test/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen_test.dart` renders the screen without overflow and finds chart area, trade ticket, and tabs.
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes with no errors or warnings.
- [ ] `flutter test` passes.
- [ ] `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` passes.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read `mockups/YouTrade.dc.html` terminal section and existing theme/providers.
- [ ] Create `lib/presentation/providers/trading_terminal_provider.dart` with immutable `TradingTerminalState` and `TradingTerminalNotifier` for tab, side, order type, leverage, size percent, and timeframe.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/symbol_header.dart` with symbol, class chip, name/venue, price, 24h change, and 4-column stat strip.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/time_frame_selector.dart` for timeframe chips.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/candlestick_chart.dart` that watches `candlesProvider`, converts domain candles newest-to-oldest, computes MA7/MA25, and renders `Candlesticks` with app-matching style.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/order_book_panel.dart` that watches `orderBookStreamProvider`, shows asks/bids with depth bars and spread.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/trade_ticket.dart` with side toggle, order-type chips, leverage slider, size-percent chips, order size/cost summary, and submit button.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/signal_gauge.dart` with SVG-style gauge, verdict, oscillator table, moving-average table, and pivot grid.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/fundamentals_card.dart` with tag cards and stat grid.
- [ ] Create `lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/lower_tabs.dart` for the tab bar.
- [ ] Implement `lib/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen.dart` composing the above widgets in a scrollable layout.
- [ ] Add `test/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen_test.dart` overriding `marketDataRepositoryProvider` with a deterministic fake repository.
- [ ] Run verification commands and fix any issues.

## Key Considerations

**Empty / Error States**
- `candlesProvider` error or empty list must show a loading/error placeholder, not a blank chart.
- Empty order book must display dashes for spread and zero-depth bars.

**Async Data**
- All market providers are asynchronous; use `AsyncValue` widgets and avoid blocking the UI.
- Widget test must override the repository so no real network/WebSocket calls are made.

**Overflow Avoidance**
- The terminal content is tall; wrap the body in a scrollable column and keep nested flex depths shallow.
- Use `LayoutBuilder` or `Expanded` carefully inside scrollable parents to avoid unbounded constraints.

**Leverage Bounds**
- Slider clamps between 1x and 100x; display as integer.

**Order Cost Calculation**
- Cost is approximate (`price * size / leverage`) and computed locally for UI preview only.

## Anti-patterns
- No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases; all market data flows through repository providers.
- No giant exchange interface changes; keep existing source contracts.
- No TODO stubs for core widgets.
- No polling for real-time data; use existing stream providers.
- No ad-hoc state mutation outside `TradingTerminalNotifier`.
- No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.
- No real network calls in widget tests; always override the repository.

title: YouTrade Flutter app: multi-venue trading terminal
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Build a Flutter mobile trading terminal called YouTrade with the screens and visual design pixel-for-pixel faithful to /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html.
- Reproduce exactly these screens: Home / Aggregated Portfolio, Trading Terminal, Markets / Screener, Exchange Detail, Options Chain, Compare, Orders & History, Account / Settings.
- Preserve the visual identity: iOS 26 Liquid Glass-style device frame, dark-first palette, Flux (dir:b) and Carbon (dir:a) visual directions, Geist/Space Grotesk/JetBrains Mono typography.
- Reproduce every interactive component from the mockups: iOS status bar, app header with theme/direction toggles, bottom tab navigation, exchange allocation bar, exchange cards, position list, equity curve canvas, symbol chips, candlestick chart with MA7/MA25 and volume, time-frame selector, compare chart, order book with depth bars, trade ticket with buy/sell toggle / order-type chips / leverage slider / size-percent buttons, lower tabs (Trade/Book/Info/Signals), technical-signals gauge, oscillator/MA/pivot tables, fundamentals card, options chain grid, market screener with filters and sparklines, orders tabs (Open/History/Positions), settings list with connected exchanges and appearance toggles.
- Follow SOLID architecture: domain abstractions, per-exchange implementations, Riverpod state layer, dumb UI.
- Support mock-first offline data and real public market data online; no exchange API secrets in the mobile app for this epic.
- Integrate public REST + WebSocket market data from Binance, Bybit, OKX, and Coinbase via direct Flutter calls.
- Add local biometric/PIN gate before portfolio/trading screens; no cloud account required.
- Keep build under ten minutes: flutter test, flutter analyze, flutter format must pass.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All mockup screens exist and are navigable with the same layout, spacing, typography, and colors as /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html.
- [ ] Domain entities, source contracts, repository, and use cases are implemented and unit-tested.
- [ ] At least two exchanges stream real tickers/candles/order books/trades through a unified abstraction.
- [ ] Offline mode displays mock data with a visible Demo/Offline indicator.
- [ ] Theme matches mockups: dark first, Flux/Carbon toggle, iOS-first styling.
- [ ] Local auth gate blocks access until biometric/PIN succeeds.
- [ ] flutter test, flutter analyze, flutter format --set-exit-if-changed lib test pass.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app (security: first build is public data only).
- No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases (architecture: must go through repository/interfaces).
- No giant exchange interface that forces every venue to implement unsupported methods (SOLID: interface segregation).
- No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contract (completeness: core abstraction must be real).
- No polling for real-time data; use WebSocket streams for active symbols (performance: respect rate limits).
- No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers (state management: emit new state).
- No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval (design fidelity).

## Approach
Adopt layered/clean architecture driven by SOLID. Domain defines small contracts (TickerSource, CandleSource, OrderBookSource, TradeSource, MarketStreamSource) and a MarketDataRepository. Each exchange provides a REST client, WebSocket client, and adapter that transform exchange DTOs into domain entities. A registry maps venues to clients so new exchanges can be added without editing core logic. Riverpod providers expose AsyncValue/Stream states to dumb UI widgets. Drift caches market data; mock data store provides offline/demo fallback. UI is implemented screen-by-screen to match the mockups, with a shared theme extension and reusable widget kit.

## Child Epics
- youtrade-0z9: Phase 1 - Scaffold + domain entities + SOLID contracts
- youtrade-fl6: Phase 2 - Data layer (mock store, cache, exchange clients)
- youtrade-qqg: Phase 3 - Presentation, theme, and auth guard
- youtrade-mv8: Phase 4 - UI screens from mockups
- youtrade-4hy: Exchange Management screen
- youtrade-75g: Phase 5 - Integration, navigation, and verification

## Architecture
- Domain: entities (Ticker, Candle, OrderBook, Trade, Symbol, Venue), source contracts, MarketDataRepository interface, use cases, ExchangeCapability registry.
- Data: per-exchange REST clients, WebSocket clients, DTOs, adapters, MarketDataRepositoryImpl, Drift cache, mock data store, secure settings.
- Presentation: Riverpod providers and notifiers for tickers, candles, order books, portfolio, theme, auth guard.
- UI: screens matching mockups exactly, reusable widgets, candlestick charts (candlesticks), sparklines (fl_chart).

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The mockups describe a sophisticated multi-venue terminal but contain only synthetic client-side data. We need a real architecture that can graduate from prototype to production without rewriting everything.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html:1-1113 contains the full interactive prototype with screens, theme tokens, component specs, and synthetic data model.
- /mockups/colors_and_type.css:1-301 defines OpenTreasury/YouTrade design tokens (fonts, brand colors, light/dark variables).
- /mockups/ios-frame.jsx:1-349 provides iOS 26 Liquid Glass frame primitives to mirror in Flutter.

**External:**
- CCXT has no Dart/Flutter SDK (docs.ccxt.com, github.com/ccxt/ccxt/issues/4861). Direct public REST/WebSocket calls from Flutter are the chosen path for this epic.
- Binance, Bybit, OKX, Coinbase all expose public REST + WebSocket market data without API keys.
- Recommended packages: riverpod, web_socket_channel, http, candlesticks, fl_chart, drift, local_auth, flutter_secure_storage, go_router.

### Approaches Considered
1. **Direct public REST/WebSocket from Flutter** (chosen)
   - Pros: No backend to run, faster prototype, matches user choice.
   - Cons: Each exchange has different formats; requires adapters.
   - Chosen because: aligns with hybrid prototype + real data scope.

2. **CCXT backend proxy**
   - Pros: Unified, secrets stay server-side.
   - Cons: Requires backend deployment; overkill for public-data-only first build.
   - Rejected because: out of scope for this epic.

3. **Use exchange-specific Dart packages (e.g. babel_binance)**
   - Pros: Less parsing code.
   - Cons: Limited exchange coverage, inconsistent APIs, dependency risk.
   - Rejected because: does not satisfy multi-venue requirement cleanly.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- All mockup screens and navigation with the same layout, components, and visual design.
- Domain architecture, per-exchange REST/WebSocket clients, adapters.
- Mock-first offline mode + real online public market data.
- Theme system (Flux/Carbon, dark/light), local auth gate.

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Real trading (create/cancel orders, balances from private APIs).
- User accounts, cloud sync, push notifications.
- Real portfolio P&L from exchange positions.
- Options chain real data (will be mock/synthetic).

### Open Questions
- Which exact timeframes to support for candles? (decide during implementation)
- Should the app support horizontal orientation or stay portrait-only? (decide during implementation)
- How many candles to cache locally? (decide during implementation)
close_reason: YouTrade Flutter multi-venue trading terminal implemented, verified, and dual-gate approved.

design:
## Goal
Update lib/ui/screens/markets_screen.dart and supporting widgets (lib/ui/widgets/markets/*) to match the mockup Markets/Screener screen exactly per docs/mockup_audit.md and docs/design_tokens.md.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Markets screen renders without an internal AppBar; search bar is the first visible element.
- [ ] Search bar has exact mockup styling: height 38px, radius 8px, border 1px line, bg card, 15px magnifier icon, placeholder 'Search symbols, venues, assets' in fg3, input text 13px fg.
- [ ] Filter chips show All, Crypto, Stocks, Futures, Options (not Forex/Equities/Commodities).
- [ ] Active filter chip uses fg/bg colors (white/dark); inactive uses transparent bg, fg2 text, line border.
- [ ] List header shows 'Symbol' / 'Last · 24h' in JetBrains Mono 8.5px fg3 uppercase.
- [ ] Market rows display exact mockup symbols (BTC, ETH, SOL, AAPL, GC, NVDA, XRP, CL, TSLA, BTC-28K-C), names, class badges (PERP/SPOT/STOCK/FUT/OPT), venues (BIN/BYB/OKX/CB), sparklines, prices, and 24h changes.
- [ ] Prices match deterministic values from DeterministicMarketDataStore for BTC/ETH/SOL/AAPL/GC=F, with exact decimals and comma separators.
- [ ] Options row (BTC-28K-C) routes to /markets/options/BTC-28K-C.
- [ ] Non-options rows route to /trading?symbol=<rawSymbol> (e.g. BTCUSDT, GC=F, XRPUSDT).
- [ ] Search and filter behavior correctly narrow the list.
- [ ] All existing and new widget tests pass; integration test verifies Markets tab values and navigation.
- [ ] flutter analyze, flutter test, and dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test integration_test tool all pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Refactor AssetClass to distinguish row badge (PERP/SPOT/STOCK/FUT/OPT) from filter category (Crypto/Stocks/Futures/Options).
- [ ] Replace provider mock data with deterministic computed values plus hardcoded extras matching mockup exactly.
- [ ] Add DeterministicMarketDataStore helpers for synchronous screener ticker/sparkline access.
- [ ] Update MarketListTile for exact typography, badge colors, price formatting with commas, and navigation.
- [ ] Replace SparklineChart with exact 46x24 line painter (no fill, 1.4 width, last 30 closes).
- [ ] Update FilterChips for All/Crypto/Stocks/Futures/Options and active/inactive mockup colors.
- [ ] Update MarketsScreen to remove AppBar, use card color for search/list, and apply exact spacing.
- [ ] Update widget tests in test/ui/screens/markets_screen_test.dart and test/ui/widgets/markets/market_list_tile_test.dart.
- [ ] Update integration test in integration_test/markets_flow_test.dart to verify exact values and options navigation.

## Key Considerations

**Deterministic Data Edge Cases**
- GC=F uses rawSymbol override; base='GC', quote='F' satisfies TradingSymbol validation.
- Symbols with no deterministic candles (NVDA, XRPUSDT, CL=F, TSLA, BTC-28K-C) use hardcoded mockup values.
- Sparklines only for the five deterministic rows; extras render no sparkline.

**Formatting Edge Cases**
- Price formatting must add comma separators and honor per-symbol decimals (1 or 2 for most, 4 for XRP and BTC-28K-C).
- 24h change must always show sign and two decimals.

**Theme Edge Cases**
- PERP badge color uses accent; SPOT uses up; STOCK/FUT/OPT use fixed mockup colors (#8b9cf0, #ffc457, #c9a6ff).
- Active filter chip uses fg/onSurface inverted colors, not primary.

**Navigation Edge Cases**
- /markets and /markets/compare must remain public per epic anti-patterns.
- /markets/options/:symbol route already exists; ensure options row uses it.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No visual approximations.
- ❌ No new dependencies (intl, flutter_svg) unless approved.
- ❌ No hardcoded API keys.
- ❌ No breaking existing navigation or auth behavior.
- ❌ No skipping tests; TDD required.
- ❌ No TODOs without issue numbers.

title: Build Compare screen from mockups
design:
## Goal
Implement the Compare screen from the YouTrade mockups as a standalone Scaffold. Use static mock comparison data and fl_chart to render two normalized price series, symbol selector chips, a time range selector, and correlation/ratio/return/volatility stats.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] lib/ui/screens/compare_screen.dart exists and renders a scrollable Compare page matching the mockup card style and dark theme.
- [ ] lib/ui/widgets/compare/ contains symbol_selector.dart, compare_chart.dart, stat_card.dart, plus time_range_selector.dart if needed.
- [ ] The compare chart uses fl_chart LineChart to plot normalized percentage series for the selected symbols.
- [ ] Tapping symbol chips and time range chips updates the selected state and re-renders the chart/stats.
- [ ] test/ui/screens/compare_screen_test.dart passes, verifying the screen renders without overflow and shows CompareChart and SymbolSelector.
- [ ] flutter analyze, flutter test, and dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test all pass.
- [ ] git commit with message \"feat(youtrade-ocj): build Compare screen from mockups\".

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read /mockups/YouTrade.dc.html Compare section (lines 449-477) for visual structure.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/screens/compare_screen.dart as StatelessWidget/Scaffold using AppTheme colors and AppColorTheme.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/compare/symbol_selector.dart: horizontal scrollable chips, supports selecting/deselecting symbols, max two selected for the two-series chart.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/compare/time_range_selector.dart: horizontal chips for 1D/1W/1M/3M/1Y, updates selected range.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/compare/compare_chart.dart: fl_chart LineChart plotting normalized percentage returns, themed grid/border.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/compare/stat_card.dart and a stats table showing correlation, ratio, return, volatility for selected symbols.
- [ ] Add static mock comparison data inside compare_screen.dart or a small local model.
- [ ] Add test/ui/screens/compare_screen_test.dart using WidgetTester, MaterialApp with AppTheme.dark(AppVisualDirection.flux), checking CompareChart and SymbolSelector exist and no overflow on 390x844.
- [ ] Run flutter test, flutter analyze, dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test; fix issues.
- [ ] Commit and close br issue.

## Key Considerations
**Mock Data Only**: No exchange API calls; all series and stats are hard-coded.
**Two-Series Limit**: Keep chart readable; selector enforces exactly two selected symbols.
**Theme Consistency**: Use colorScheme.surface, appColors.borderSubtle, appColors.accent, bullish/bearish colors.
**Overflow Safety**: Wrap selector rows in SingleChildScrollView; screen body scrolls; chart has fixed height.
**Accessibility**: Chip labels and stat labels use existing text theme; no custom colors that fail contrast.

## Anti-patterns
- No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases (architecture: must go through repository/interfaces).
- No giant exchange interface that forces every venue to implement unsupported methods (SOLID: interface segregation).
- No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contract.
- No polling for real-time data; use WebSocket streams for active symbols.
- No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers.
- No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.

title: Remove tautological tests from youtrade-n97
description:
## Goal
Remove tests that pass by copying implementation logic or asserting compiler-guaranteed truths.

## Tests to Remove
- test/data/auth/secure_pin_auth_service_test.dart:253-261 — 'uses SHA-256 for hashing' duplicates `_hashPin` implementation (`sha256.convert(utf8.encode('\$salt\$pin')).toString()`).

## Why This Is RED
The test computes the expected hash using the exact same expression the production code uses. If a bug is introduced in the concatenation order, encoding, or algorithm, the test will duplicate the bug and still pass. Correct PIN authentication is already covered by 'returns true for the correct PIN' (line 184-188), so removing this test does not lose bug-catching ability.

## Effort Estimate
1 hour

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Listed test deleted
- [ ] No new tautological tests introduced
- [ ] `flutter test` (or local equivalent) still passes
- [ ] Coverage drop, if any, is accepted because the removed test provided false confidence

## Anti-patterns
- Do not keep tests 'just in case'
- Do not replace with equally meaningless assertions
- Do not re-implement production logic inside a test to compute expected values

title: Add missing corner case tests for OKX and Coinbase clients
description:
## Goal
Cover error paths and boundary conditions in the new OKX/Coinbase REST and WebSocket clients.

## Missing Tests

### OKX REST (`lib/data/datasources/remote/okx/okx_rest_client.dart`)
- `fetchOrderBook` non-200 response and API error code (`code != '0'`) → exact `NetworkFailure` message
- `fetchTrades` API error code response
- `fetchCandles` API error code response
- `fetchCandles` / `fetchOrderBook` / `fetchTrades` network exception (currently only ticker is covered)
- `fetchTicker`/`fetchCandles`/`fetchOrderBook`/`fetchTrades` with `open24h == 0` → `change24hPercent` must be `0.0`
- `fetchOrderBook` with empty bids/asks arrays → returns empty `OrderBook` without error
- `fetchTrades` with unknown/empty side string → maps to `TradeSide.sell`
- `fetchTicker` with missing/empty timestamp → falls back to `DateTime.now().toUtc()` (assert within a tolerance)

### OKX WebSocket (`lib/data/datasources/remote/okx/okx_websocket_client.dart`)
- Non-relevant message ignored (e.g., subscription ack with no `data` list)
- `watchOrderBook` and `watchTrades` stream errors emit `UnknownFailure`
- Connection failure (`channel.ready` rejection) emits `UnknownFailure` and closes controller

### Coinbase REST (`lib/data/datasources/remote/coinbase/coinbase_rest_client.dart`)
- `fetchOrderBook` non-200 and `ParseFailure` on malformed JSON
- `fetchTrades` non-200
- `fetchCandles` malformed list element / non-numeric values
- `fetchCandles` / `fetchOrderBook` / `fetchTrades` network exception
- `fetchTicker` volume fallback when quote `volume` is null but stats `volume` is present
- `fetchTicker` with `open == '0'` → `change24hPercent` must be `0.0`

### Coinbase WebSocket (`lib/data/datasources/remote/coinbase/coinbase_websocket_client.dart`)
- `watchOrderBook` and `watchTrades` stream errors emit `UnknownFailure`
- Connection failure path emits `UnknownFailure` and closes controller

## Effort Estimate
8-12 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each missing corner case has a RED-GREEN test (fails before fix/passes after, or currently fails if bug exists)
- [ ] Assertions verify exact failure type/message or exact parsed values
- [ ] No test passes simply because the mock returned the expected value

## Key Considerations
- Mock only external HTTP/WebSocket transport, never the parser under test.
- For `DateTime.now()` fallbacks, assert the returned timestamp is within 1 second of UTC now.
- For API-error-code paths, assert both the failure type and the message prefix to ensure the context string is correct.

## Anti-patterns
- Do not write tests that pass immediately without exercising the corner case
- Do not mock the code under test
- Do not assert only `isA<ParseFailure>()` without checking the message describes the real failure
close_reason: Added corner case tests for OKX/Coinbase REST and WebSocket clients covering API error codes, network exceptions, boundary values, malformed data, stream errors, and connection failures. Full verification passes.

title: Add missing auth corner case and concurrency tests
description:
## Goal
Cover edge cases in PIN/biometric authentication and notifier state machine.

## Missing Tests

### SecurePinAuthService (`lib/data/auth/secure_pin_auth_service.dart`)
- `setPin` with a PIN longer than 4 digits (e.g., '123456') → should succeed; verify stored hash length and salt unchanged
- `authenticatePin` with a PIN containing non-digit characters (e.g., '12a4') → returns false
- Concurrent `setPin` calls from multiple isolates/futures → deterministic final state, no duplicate salts
- `authenticatePin` when stored hash exists but salt is an empty string → returns false (already covered by missing-salt test; add empty-string variant)

### AuthNotifier (`lib/presentation/auth/auth_notifier.dart`)
- `initialize` when `canCheckBiometrics` returns true but no PIN is set → must emit `AuthUnauthenticated(pinSet: false)`, not attempt biometrics
- `authenticateWithBiometrics` when PIN is removed between check and call → should still attempt local auth and succeed/fail based on result
- `signOut` during an active `authenticateWithPin` call → state ends unauthenticated after async work completes
- Multiple rapid `signOut` calls do not throw or emit extra states

### AuthGateScreen (`lib/ui/auth/auth_gate_screen.dart`)
- Pasting a multi-line or whitespace-only string into the PIN field → treated as invalid
- Very long pasted input respects `maxLength` and does not overflow
- Biometric error recovery: after biometric failure, PIN entry still works

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each corner case test fails for the right reason before the production behavior is confirmed
- [ ] Tests use real production logic or realistic fakes; mocks only stand in for platform plugins
- [ ] Each test names the specific bug it prevents

## Key Considerations
- The `FakePinAuthService` is acceptable for notifier tests because it mirrors the `PinAuthService` contract, but assertions must be on `AuthState` emissions, not the fake's internal state.
- For concurrent tests, use `Future.wait` and verify the final PIN hash/salt deterministically.

## Anti-patterns
- Do not test the fake implementation instead of production
- Do not assert only that no exception was thrown
- Do not duplicate production logic inside assertions
close_reason: Added auth corner case and concurrency tests for SecurePinAuthService, AuthNotifier, and AuthGateScreen. Made AuthNotifier.signOut idempotent and cancel ongoing authenticateWithPin. Full verification passes.

title: Validate youtrade-n97 test improvements with mutation testing
description:
## Manual Mutation Log

No Dart mutation testing tool was integrated (none available in pub ecosystem without significant setup). Performed targeted manual mutations on critical paths and verified the suite killed them.

| # | Target | Mutation | Killing Test(s) | Result |
|---|--------|----------|-----------------|--------|
| 1 | OKXRestClient._parseTicker | `change24hPercent = 0.0` always | `fetchTicker returns Success on valid response` (expects ~1.01%) | KILLED |
| 2 | CoinbaseRestClient._buildTicker | Always use `stats['volume']`, ignore `quote['volume']` | `fetchTicker prefers quote volume over stats volume` | KILLED |
| 3 | SecurePinAuthService._hashPin | Reverse concatenation to `$pin$salt` | `stores SHA-256 of salt followed by PIN` | KILLED |
| 4 | MarketDataRepositoryImpl._fetchWithCache | Remove stale-cache fallback on source error | `getTicker/getCandles/getOrderBook/getTrades falls back to stale cache on network failure` | KILLED |

All mutations were reverted immediately after verification. No surviving mutants in critical paths.
close_reason: Manual mutation testing completed. All 4 targeted mutants in OKX ticker, Coinbase volume, PIN hashing, and stale-cache fallback were killed by the test suite. No surviving mutants in critical paths. Log added to task description.

title: Build Exchange Detail screen from mockups
design:
## Goal
Build the Exchange Detail screen from the YouTrade mockups as a standalone Scaffold using the existing theme system. The screen must display venue logo/name, allocation percentage, connected/API status, balance summary, asset balances, 24h P&L, recent trades, and API key status using static mock data.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] lib/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen.dart exists and compiles as a standalone Scaffold.
- [ ] All reusable widgets live under lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/ and use only the existing theme/extensions and Venue enum.
- [ ] Screen renders venue name, balance, 24h P&L, API status, asset balances, and recent trades with static mock data.
- [ ] test/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen_test.dart passes and verifies the screen renders without overflow and shows venue name, balance, and status.
- [ ] flutter analyze passes with zero warnings/errors.
- [ ] flutter test passes.
- [ ] dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test passes.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read mockups/YouTrade.dc.html exchange-detail section to extract layout, spacing, typography, and colors.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen.dart with static mock data for Venue.binance.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/venue_header.dart for venue dot, name, kinds label.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/api_status_banner.dart for API LIVE indicator and API key status.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/balance_card.dart and pnl_card.dart for the summary row.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/asset_balance_tile.dart for each asset with share bar.
- [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/exchange_detail/trade_history_list.dart for recent trades.
- [ ] Compose screen with SafeArea, SingleChildScrollView, and horizontal scrollable venue chips.
- [ ] Add test/ui/screens/exchange_detail_screen_test.dart with MaterialApp wrapper and overflow assertion.
- [ ] Run flutter test, flutter analyze, and dart format; fix any issues.
- [ ] Commit with feat(youtrade-tj9): build Exchange Detail screen from mockups.

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Overflow**
- The screen must scroll vertically and the venue chip row must scroll horizontally to avoid RenderFlex overflow on small devices.
- Balance/PnL cards use Expanded inside a Row so they share available width.

**Edge Case: Theme/Direction**
- Widgets must read Theme.of(context) and AppColorTheme extension; no hard-coded colors.
- Light/dark and Flux/Carbon directions are handled by the existing theme system.

**Edge Case: Empty Lists**
- Static mock data always includes at least one asset and one trade, but widgets should not crash with empty lists.

**Edge Case: Text Fitting**
- Large balance values use FittedBox or font sizing to avoid overflow.

## Anti-patterns
- No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases; business logic goes through repository/interfaces.
- No giant exchange interface forcing unsupported methods.
- No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contracts.
- No polling for real-time data; this screen uses static mock data only.
- No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers.
- No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.

design:
## Goal
Align lib/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen.dart and lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/* to the Trading Terminal mockup in mockups/YouTrade.dc.html with pixel-perfect styling, deterministic mock data, and full test coverage.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Symbol chips show BTC, ETH, SOL, AAPL, GOLD with exact active/inactive colors, borders, radii, and typography.
- [ ] Price header matches mockup: Space Grotesk 19px symbol, class tag, name/venue, JetBrains Mono 24px last price in up/down color, 24h change.
- [ ] 24h stat strip renders High/Low/Vol/Funding with exact grid styling; Funding hidden for SPOT/EQUITY classes.
- [ ] Chart toolbar has timeframe chips (1m,5m,15m,1h,4h,1d,1w) and a compare button that navigates to /markets/compare.
- [ ] Candlestick chart is custom-painted to match mockup: 248px height, grid, MA7 (#00e6d2 90%), MA25 (#ffb020), volume bars at 32% opacity, bull/bear colors, dashed last-price line + label, dashed crosshair + label, right price axis.
- [ ] Lower tabs Trade/Book/Info/Signals match exact active/inactive styling.
- [ ] Trade tab: Buy/Long & Sell/Short toggle, Limit/Market/Stop order-type chips, price row, leverage slider hidden for non-perp/future symbols, 25/50/75/100 size-percent buttons, size/cost estimate, submit CTA that shows a demo confirmation dialog.
- [ ] Book tab: asks (red) and bids (green) with cumulative depth bars, mid price strip, spread text.
- [ ] Info tab: tag cards, 8-item stats grid, About paragraph using DeterministicMarketDataStore metadata.
- [ ] Signals tab: gauge with 85% opacity arcs and needle, BUY/NEUTRAL/SELL verdict, oscillator list, moving-average list, pivot levels grid — all values computed deterministically from candles/ticker.
- [ ] DeterministicMarketDataStore is wired as the repository mock fallback so runtime data matches the mockup exactly.
- [ ] All existing widget tests pass; new tests verify exact deterministic values, tab switching, compare navigation, and demo-trade dialog.
- [ ] Existing integration test updated to assert terminal exact values and compare navigation.
- [ ] flutter analyze, flutter test, and dart format --set-exit-if-changed all pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] 1. Wire DeterministicMarketDataStore: replace MockMarketDataStore default in MarketDataRepositoryImpl and update repository tests if needed.
- [ ] 2. Add TradingSymbol metadata helper (symbol_class.dart) mapping raw symbols to name, class, base, decimals, venue per mockup symMeta().
- [ ] 3. Update formatting.dart: add formatAxisNumber, formatSynthetics, ensure grouping matches mockup.
- [ ] 4. Update symbol_chip_row.dart: swap chips to BTC/ETH/SOL/AAPL/GOLD, apply exact active/inactive styles.
- [ ] 5. Update symbol_header.dart: exact price header layout/stat strip; hide funding for non-perp/future.
- [ ] 6. Update time_frame_selector.dart: exact chip styles; compare button navigates to /markets/compare via context.go.
- [ ] 7. Replace candlestick_chart.dart with custom CustomPaint implementation matching mockup drawing logic.
- [ ] 8. Update lower_tabs.dart: exact active bar and text colors.
- [ ] 9. Update trade_ticket.dart: exact side/order/size/leverage/submit styling; demo confirmation AlertDialog; hide leverage for spot/equity.
- [ ] 10. Update order_book_panel.dart: exact book row styling, depth bars, mid/spread strip.
- [ ] 11. Update fundamentals_card.dart: exact tags/stats/about per mockup fundamentals().
- [ ] 12. Update signal_gauge.dart: exact gauge, oscillators, MAs, pivots per mockup screenVals TA section.
- [ ] 13. Update trading_terminal_screen.dart: remove AppBar title per mockup full-screen style (keep Scaffold body); ensure safe area padding.
- [ ] 14. Update widget tests: deterministic repository override, exact value assertions, tab switching, compare navigation, submit dialog.
- [ ] 15. Update integration test helpers and app_test with deterministic repo and terminal assertions.
- [ ] 16. Run flutter analyze, flutter test, dart format --set-exit-if-changed.

## Key Considerations

**Deterministic Data Dependency**:
- DeterministicMarketDataStore uses the same Park-Miller PRNG and series() algorithm as the HTML mockup. Wiring it as the repository fallback guarantees runtime values match the mockup screenshots.
- Repository tests that assert MockMarketDataStore-specific values must be updated to DeterministicMarketDataStore expectations.

**Symbol Classes**:
- BTCUSDT/ETHUSDT/SOLUSDT are crypto; AAPL is equity; GC=F is future. Funding applies only to PERP/FUTURE classes per mockup (hide for SPOT/EQUITY).
- Leverage slider is shown only for PERP/FUTURE classes.

**Candlestick Chart Custom Painter**:
- The `candlesticks` package cannot reproduce the mockup's exact grid, dashed last-price line, crosshair, and right price axis. Implement a CustomPaint widget replicating the HTML drawCandles() logic.
- Use `withValues(alpha: ...)` for translucent colors; avoid deprecated `withOpacity`.

**Compare Navigation**:
- Compare is a public route (/markets/compare). Use `GoRouter.of(context).go('/markets/compare')` or `context.go`.
- Existing public routes set: {'/markets', '/markets/compare'} in app_router.dart — no new route needed.

**Demo Confirmation**:
- Submit CTA shows an AlertDialog with side, symbol, price, size, and cost; no real order execution.

**Edge Cases**:
- Empty candles/ticker: show placeholders/zeros without crashing.
- Invalid symbol parameter: retain existing fallback to selectedSymbolProvider.
- Theme brightness: use colorScheme and AppColorTheme; all colors resolve at runtime for dark/light and flux/carbon.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No hardcoded colors outside theme tokens / design_tokens.md.
- ❌ No new dependencies (use existing packages only: flutter, riverpod, go_router, fl_chart if needed, candlesticks replaced by CustomPaint).
- ❌ No breaking existing navigation or auth behavior.
- ❌ No skipping tests; write failing tests before production code (TDD).
- ❌ No real trading logic in submit handler.
- ❌ No hardcoded API keys.

## Goal
Build the Portfolio/Home screen from the YouTrade.dc.html mockup as a standalone Scaffold, matching the dark-first design system and using existing Riverpod theme providers. The screen displays aggregated net worth, an equity curve, allocation by venue, exchange cards, and open positions.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `lib/ui/screens/portfolio_screen.dart` exists and renders without overflow on a 390x844 iPhone-sized viewport.
- [ ] Screen matches mockup sections: iOS-style status bar, app header with theme/direction toggles, total equity, 24h delta, equity curve, allocation bar, exchange cards, and open positions list.
- [ ] Reusable widgets live only under `lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/` (`exchange_card.dart`, `position_tile.dart`, `equity_curve.dart`, `allocation_bar.dart`).
- [ ] Uses `flutter_riverpod` and existing theme extensions (`AppColorTheme`, `appThemeProvider`, `themeSettingsProvider`).
- [ ] `test/ui/screens/portfolio_screen_test.dart` passes and verifies: total equity shown, allocation bar present, at least one exchange card rendered, at least one position tile rendered, no overflow exceptions.
- [ ] `flutter test`, `flutter analyze`, and `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test` all pass.

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read `mockups/YouTrade.dc.html` to extract layout, colors, typography, and spacing for the Home/Portfolio screen.
- [ ] Create reusable widgets:
  - `lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/allocation_bar.dart` — segmented horizontal bar with exchange colors and share percentages.
  - `lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/exchange_card.dart` — venue avatar, name, kinds, live dot, value, and percentage.
  - `lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/position_tile.dart` — symbol avatar, symbol/side badge, venue/qty, value, PnL.
  - `lib/ui/widgets/portfolio/equity_curve.dart` — mock equity line chart using `fl_chart` with 1D/1W/1M/1Y/ALL range chips.
- [ ] Implement `lib/ui/screens/portfolio_screen.dart`:
  - `ConsumerWidget` or `ConsumerStatefulWidget` observing `themeSettingsProvider`.
  - Static mock portfolio data (no real network calls or exchange secrets).
  - Header toggles call `ref.read(themeSettingsProvider.notifier).toggleLightDark()` and `toggleVisualDirection()`.
  - Scrollable body with all mockup sections.
  - Placeholder bottom tab bar row (Phase 5 integrates real navigation).
- [ ] Write `test/ui/screens/portfolio_screen_test.dart` using `ProviderScope` and `MaterialApp` with `appThemeProvider`.
- [ ] Run verification commands and fix analyzer/formatting issues.
- [ ] Commit with the specified message.

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Empty or missing providers**
- If a required provider is missing, use static mock data widgets instead of introducing new production dependencies.
- Do not create TODO stubs for domain entities or repository contracts.

**Edge Case: Theme/direction changes**
- Toggles must update `themeSettingsProvider` state; the widget must rebuild via `ConsumerWidget`/`ref.watch`.
- Test should run with default dark Flux theme to avoid golden failures.

**Edge Case: Layout overflow**
- Wrap screen body in `SingleChildScrollView`.
- Avoid unbounded vertical growth in lists by using `Column` inside scroll view (all mock items fixed).
- Test on 390x844 logical viewport to catch overflow.

**Edge Case: Accessibility**
- Use `IconButton`/`TextButton` semantics for toggles and tappable cards.
- Provide `Tooltip` for icon-only header actions.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No exchange API secrets or private keys stored in the Flutter app.
- ❌ No direct exchange-specific logic in UI or use cases (architecture: must go through repository/interfaces).
- ❌ No giant exchange interface that forces every venue to implement unsupported methods (SOLID: interface segregation).
- ❌ No TODO stubs for core domain entities or repository contract.
- ❌ No polling for real-time data; use WebSocket streams for active symbols (static mock data allowed here).
- ❌ No ad-hoc state mutation outside notifiers.
- ❌ No deviation from the mockup design system without explicit user approval.
- ❌ Do not modify `lib/main.dart` or global routing.
- ❌ Do not create bottom navigation here (Phase 5 handles integration).

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: Empty open orders after cancel**
- If all open orders are cancelled, the Open tab should render an empty list without throwing.
- Cancel action must be demo-only and not call real APIs.

**Edge Case: Theme / direction switching**
- Colors must resolve from AppColorTheme so the screen works in dark Flux (default), dark Carbon, light Flux, and light Carbon.

**Edge Case: Tab state**
- Tapping a tab must update `_selectedIndex` and rebuild body.

**Edge Case: Typography precision**

**Edge Case: Integration test stability**

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ No visual approximations — every measurement, color, and font must match the mockup.
- ❌ No new dependencies unless approved.
- ❌ No hardcoded API keys or secrets.
- ❌ No breaking existing navigation or auth behavior.
- ❌ No skipping tests; TDD required.

- ❌ No production-only TODOs or stubs.

title: Wire all screens to real Bybit demo API
description:
# Wire all screens to real Bybit demo API

## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Requirement 1: All market data (tickers, candles, orderbook, trades) flows from Bybit V5 demo API at api-demo.bybit.com
- Requirement 2: Market screener fetches ALL available linear + spot pairs dynamically from /v5/market/tickers (no hardcoded symbol list)
- Requirement 3: Portfolio screen shows real wallet balance (total equity, per-coin) from demo account via authenticated /v5/account/wallet-balance
- Requirement 4: Portfolio screen shows real open positions from /v5/position/list
- Requirement 5: Orders screen shows real open orders from /v5/order/realtime and history from /v5/order/history
- Requirement 6: HMAC-SHA256 request signing implemented for all authenticated endpoints
- Requirement 7: .env credentials (BYBIT_DEMO_API_KEY, BYBIT_DEMO_API_SECRET) loaded via flutter_dotenv
- Requirement 8: WebSocket streams use wss://stream-demo.bybit.com for real-time ticker/orderbook/trade data
- Requirement 9: BybitRestClient uses category=linear for perpetuals and category=spot for spot pairs
- Requirement 10: DeterministicMarketDataStore retained as offline fallback only
- Requirement 11: Trading terminal shows real candles, orderbook, and trades from Bybit demo

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Market screener displays hundreds of real Bybit pairs with live prices and 24h change
- [ ] Portfolio screen shows real demo account wallet balance (USDT, BTC, etc.)
- [ ] Portfolio screen shows real open positions with live PnL
- [ ] Orders screen shows real open orders and order history from demo account
- [ ] Trading terminal shows real candlestick chart, order book, and recent trades
- [ ] All unit/widget tests passing
- [ ] All integration tests passing on iOS Simulator
- [ ] App runs on iOS Simulator with real Bybit demo data
- [ ] No hardcoded mock prices in production data path
- [ ] flutter analyze clean

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO hardcoded symbol lists in screener (completeness: must show all available Bybit pairs)
- NO mock portfolio data in production path (accuracy: must show real demo account balance)
- NO storing API secrets in source code (security: must load from .env at runtime)
- NO skipping HMAC signing on authenticated endpoints (security: Bybit requires valid signatures)
- NO using production api.bybit.com URLs (correctness: must use api-demo.bybit.com)
- NO removing offline fallback (resilience: DeterministicMarketDataStore must remain for offline mode)

## Approach
Make Bybit the sole data source. Switch base URLs to api-demo.bybit.com. Add BybitAccountClient for authenticated endpoints with HMAC-SHA256 signing. Replace marketScreenerProvider with a live fetcher that queries /v5/market/tickers for all linear + spot pairs. Replace portfolioDataProvider with real wallet balance and positions. Replace orders data with real open orders and history. Load demo credentials from .env via flutter_dotenv.

## Architecture
- lib/data/datasources/remote/bybit/bybit_account_client.dart - New authenticated client (HMAC signing, wallet/positions/orders)
- lib/data/datasources/remote/bybit/bybit_rest_client.dart - Update base URL to demo, fix category mapping
- lib/data/datasources/remote/bybit/bybit_websocket_client.dart - Update WS URL to demo stream
- lib/core/bybit_config.dart - Load credentials from .env
- lib/presentation/providers/market_screener_provider.dart - Rewrite to fetch all pairs from Bybit
- lib/presentation/providers/portfolio_data_provider.dart - Rewrite with real wallet/positions
- lib/presentation/providers/orders_provider.dart - New provider for real orders
- lib/presentation/providers/repository_provider.dart - Wire BybitAccountClient

## Design Rationale
### Problem
All screens currently show hardcoded mock data. User wants real Bybit demo account data on every screen.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- lib/data/datasources/remote/bybit/bybit_rest_client.dart:23 - Already implements V5 market data, points to api.bybit.com
- lib/presentation/providers/market_screener_provider.dart:117 - Hardcoded _mockMarkets list
- lib/presentation/providers/portfolio_data_provider.dart:78 - Hardcoded DeterministicMarketDataStore data
- lib/presentation/providers/repository_provider.dart:155-191 - Multi-venue wiring already exists

**External:**
- Bybit V5 API: /v5/market/tickers returns ALL tickers per category without symbol param
- Bybit V5 API: Authenticated endpoints require X-BAPI-SIGN (HMAC-SHA256 of timestamp+apiKey+recvWindow+params)
- Demo base URL: api-demo.bybit.com (same paths as production)
- WebSocket demo: wss://stream-demo.bybit.com/v5/public/linear

### Approaches Considered
1. Bybit-First Single-Venue (chosen) - simplest, user asked for Bybit only
2. Multi-Venue - rejected (4x work, unnecessary)
3. Dart SDK - rejected (none exists)

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Bybit demo REST+WS market data, authenticated wallet/positions/orders, dynamic screener, .env loading
**Out of scope:** Other exchanges, trade execution, WebSocket private streams

### Open Questions
- Show ALL pairs or just USDT-quoted? (Default: all linear + spot)
- Portfolio equity curve: historical balance API or synthesize? (Default: synthesize from positions)
close_reason: All 6 tasks complete. All success criteria met. Both final reviewers approved. 720 tests pass, analyze clean, integration tests pass on iOS Simulator.

title: Add flutter_dotenv, BybitConfig, and switch Bybit base URLs to demo
description:
## Goal
Set up the foundation: load .env credentials, create BybitConfig, switch all Bybit REST and WebSocket base URLs to demo endpoints.

## Effort Estimate
~3-4 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - lib/data/datasources/remote/bybit/bybit_rest_client.dart:21-23 - constructor with baseUrl
   - lib/data/datasources/remote/bybit/bybit_websocket_client.dart - WS connection URL
   - lib/presentation/providers/repository_provider.dart:92-96 - BybitRestClient provider
   - lib/main.dart - app entry point

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   - test/core/bybit_config_test.dart:
     - test_loads_api_key_from_dotenv (verify exact key value from test .env)
     - test_loads_api_secret_from_dotenv (verify exact secret value)
     - test_returns_empty_strings_when_env_not_loaded (public endpoints still work)
     - test_base_url_is_demo_endpoint (verify https://api-demo.bybit.com)
     - test_ws_url_is_demo_stream (verify wss://stream-demo.bybit.com/v5/public/linear)
   - test/data/datasources/remote/bybit/bybit_rest_client_test.dart:
     - test_default_base_url_is_demo (verify URL string in constructor)

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] Add flutter_dotenv: ^5.2.1 to pubspec.yaml dependencies
   - [ ] Add .env to pubspec.yaml flutter assets list
   - [ ] Add .env to .gitignore (prevent committing secrets)
   - [ ] Create lib/core/bybit_config.dart with:
     ```dart
     class BybitConfig {
       static String get apiKey => dotenv.maybeGet("BYBIT_DEMO_API_KEY") ?? "";
       static String get apiSecret => dotenv.maybeGet("BYBIT_DEMO_API_SECRET") ?? "";
       static const String baseUrl = "https://api-demo.bybit.com";
       static const String wsLinearUrl = "wss://stream-demo.bybit.com/v5/public/linear";
       static const String wsSpotUrl = "wss://stream-demo.bybit.com/v5/public/spot";
       static bool get hasCredentials => apiKey.isNotEmpty && apiSecret.isNotEmpty;
     }
     ```
   - [ ] Update bybit_rest_client.dart default baseUrl to BybitConfig.baseUrl
   - [ ] Update bybit_websocket_client.dart default WS URL to BybitConfig.wsLinearUrl
   - [ ] Update lib/main.dart: add `await dotenv.load(fileName: ".env");` before runApp
   - [ ] Update repository_provider.dart to use BybitConfig constants for BybitRestClient/BybitWebSocketClient
   - [ ] Create test/.env with test credentials for widget/integration tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] BybitConfig.apiKey returns the value from .env BYBIT_DEMO_API_KEY
- [ ] BybitConfig.apiSecret returns the value from .env BYBIT_DEMO_API_SECRET
- [ ] BybitConfig returns empty strings when dotenv not loaded (no crash)
- [ ] BybitRestClient default baseUrl equals "https://api-demo.bybit.com"
- [ ] BybitWebSocketClient default URL equals "wss://stream-demo.bybit.com/v5/public/linear"
- [ ] .env listed in .gitignore
- [ ] .env listed in pubspec.yaml assets
- [ ] 6+ unit tests pass with specific named assertions
- [ ] flutter analyze clean (0 issues)

## Anti-Patterns
- NO committing .env with real credentials to git (security: secrets must not be in version control)
- NO logging BybitConfig.apiKey or apiSecret (security: prevent credential leakage)
- NO hardcoding credentials in source code (security: must load from .env at runtime)
- NO using production api.bybit.com (correctness: demo account requires api-demo.bybit.com)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: .env File Missing**
- dotenv.load() will throw if file not in assets. MUST use try/catch in main.dart.
- BybitConfig getters use dotenv.maybeGet() which returns null if not loaded. Falls back to empty string.
- Public endpoints (market data) work without credentials. Only authenticated endpoints need keys.

**Edge Case: Empty Credentials**
- If BYBIT_DEMO_API_KEY or SECRET not set, hasCredentials returns false.
- Portfolio/Orders screens should show "Connect API key" state instead of crashing.
- Market data screens work fine without credentials.

**Edge Case: WebSocket URL Per Category**
- Linear perpetuals: wss://stream-demo.bybit.com/v5/public/linear
- Spot: wss://stream-demo.bybit.com/v5/public/spot
- BybitWebSocketClient currently connects to a single URL. May need separate clients for linear vs spot, or parameterize the URL.
- For now, default to linear since most demo positions are USDT perps.

**Edge Case: Test Environment**
- Tests need a test .env file or dotenv stub. Create test/.env with dummy values.
- Unit tests for BybitConfig should load test/.env, not production .env.
- Integration tests on simulator use the real .env.

**Reference Implementation**
- Study how other Flutter apps use flutter_dotenv: https://pub.dev/packages/flutter_dotenv
- dotenv.load() must complete before any code reads BybitConfig
close_reason: BybitConfig, flutter_dotenv, and demo base URLs implemented

title: Add BybitAccountClient with HMAC-SHA256 signing for authenticated endpoints

Create BybitAccountClient that signs and sends authenticated requests to Bybit V5 demo API for wallet balance, positions, and orders.

## Implementation

2. Write tests first (TDD):

     - test_get_wallet_balance_signs_request_correctly (verify HMAC signature headers present)
     - test_get_positions_returns_list (mock HTTP response, verify parsing)
     - test_get_open_orders_returns_list
     - test_get_order_history_returns_list

     - test_http_error_returns_failure

3. Implementation checklist:
   - [ ] Create lib/data/datasources/remote/bybit/bybit_account_client.dart
   - [ ] Implement HMAC-SHA256 signing: timestamp + apiKey + "5000" + queryString
   - [ ] getWalletBalance() -> GET /v5/account/wallet-balance?accountType=UNIFIED
   - [ ] getPositions() -> GET /v5/position/list?category=linear&settleCoin=USDT
   - [ ] getOpenOrders() -> GET /v5/order/realtime?category=linear
   - [ ] getOrderHistory() -> GET /v5/order/history?category=linear
   - [ ] Add crypto package import for HMAC-SHA256 (already in pubspec)
   - [ ] Create domain entities: WalletBalance, Position, OpenOrder, OrderHistory
   - [ ] Return Result<T> wrapping parsed data or Failure

## Success Criteria
- [ ] BybitAccountClient signs requests with correct HMAC-SHA256 signature
- [ ] getWalletBalance parses totalEquity, per-coin walletBalance, usdValue
- [ ] getPositions parses symbol, side, size, avgPrice, unrealisedPnl, markPrice

## Anti-Patterns
- NO logging API key/secret or full signatures (security)
- NO hardcoding credentials (security: use BybitConfig)
- NO skipping signature on any authenticated call (security: Bybit rejects unsigned)
- NO using production URL (must use api-demo.bybit.com)

title: Dynamic symbol picker for Trading Terminal
description:
# Dynamic symbol picker for Trading Terminal

## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Requirement 1: The Trade screen symbol selector is a search bar with dropdown overlay, NOT hardcoded chips
- Requirement 2: The dropdown shows matching symbols from the already-fetched marketScreenerItemsProvider data (all Bybit pairs)
- Requirement 3: Each dropdown result shows symbol name, price, and 24h change
- Requirement 4: Selecting a symbol from the dropdown sets selectedSymbolProvider and loads real market data for that symbol
- Requirement 5: _symbolFromRaw() dynamically parses any USDT-quoted pair (strips USDT suffix for base, e.g. DOGEUSDT -> base: DOGE)
- Requirement 6: resolveSymbolMetadata() works dynamically for any symbol (derives name from base, decimals from price magnitude, class from asset class)
- Requirement 7: Navigating from Markets screener to terminal with any symbol (e.g. /trading?symbol=DOGEUSDT) loads correctly
- Requirement 8: Search is case-insensitive and matches both rawSymbol and display symbol
- Requirement 9: When screener data is not yet loaded (terminal opened directly), a lightweight fallback fetches tickers on demand

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Trade screen shows a search bar instead of hardcoded symbol chips
- [ ] Typing "DOGE" shows DOGEUSDT in the dropdown with live price
- [ ] Selecting any symbol from dropdown loads real ticker, candles, orderbook, trades
- [ ] Navigating from Markets screener with any symbol works correctly
- [ ] No hardcoded symbol lists in the Trade screen
- [ ] All unit/widget tests passing
- [ ] All integration tests passing on iOS Simulator
- [ ] flutter analyze clean

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO hardcoded symbol chip lists (completeness: must support any Bybit pair)
- NO extra API calls when screener data already available (efficiency: reuse existing fetch)
- NO crashing on unknown symbols (resilience: dynamic parsing must handle any USDT pair)
- NO removing the current symbol display in the terminal header (UX: keep showing selected symbol info)

## Approach
Replace the hardcoded SymbolChipRow with a search bar widget that filters marketScreenerItemsProvider data. Show a dropdown overlay with matching results (symbol, price, change). Fix _symbolFromRaw() to dynamically parse any USDT pair by stripping the quote suffix. Fix resolveSymbolMetadata() to derive metadata dynamically. Add a fallback ticker fetch for when screener data isn't loaded yet.

## Architecture
- lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/symbol_search_bar.dart - New search bar + dropdown overlay widget
- lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/symbol_chip_row.dart - Remove or replace with SymbolSearchBar
- lib/domain/entities/symbol_metadata.dart - Make resolveSymbolMetadata dynamic
- lib/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen.dart - Fix _symbolFromRaw for arbitrary symbols
- lib/presentation/providers/selected_symbol_provider.dart - No changes (already works)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The Trade screen only supports 5 hardcoded symbols. The user wants to trade any of the hundreds of Bybit pairs available on the exchange. The Markets screener already fetches all pairs but the terminal can't display arbitrary symbols.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/symbol_chip_row.dart:11-42 - Hardcoded _chipSymbols list with 5 symbols
- lib/ui/screens/trading_terminal_screen.dart:76-145 - _symbolFromRaw has hardcoded switch, default case sets base=rawSymbol (BUG)
- lib/domain/entities/symbol_metadata.dart:42-143 - resolveSymbolMetadata has hardcoded switch, fallback uses symbol.base
- lib/presentation/providers/market_screener_provider.dart:120-149 - marketScreenerItemsProvider already fetches all pairs

**External:**
- Bybit V5 API: /v5/market/tickers?category=linear returns ALL pairs in one call
- No separate search endpoint needed - client-side filtering is sufficient for ~500 pairs

### Approaches Considered
1. **Reuse Screener Data + Search Bar** (chosen)
   - Pros: Zero extra API calls, instant search, shows live prices in dropdown
   - Cons: Needs fallback if screener not loaded
   - Chosen because: Reuses existing data, simplest, fastest

2. **Dedicated Symbol Search API Call**
   - Pros: Works without screener loaded
   - Cons: Extra API call, slower, duplicates data
   - Rejected because: Unnecessary when screener already has all data

3. **Full-Screen Symbol Picker Page**
   - Pros: More screen space
   - Cons: Breaks inline trading flow, heavier UX change
   - Rejected because: Overkill, user wants inline search

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Replace symbol chips with search bar + dropdown
- Dynamic symbol parsing for any USDT pair
- Dynamic metadata resolution
- Fallback ticker fetch when screener not loaded

**Out of scope:**
- Symbol favorites/watchlist - deferred
- Recent symbols history - deferred
- Cross-venue symbol search - deferred (Bybit only)

### Open Questions
- Should the search bar show the currently selected symbol as a chip/badge? (Default: yes, show current symbol next to search bar)
- Should dropdown close on selection or stay open for quick switching? (Default: close on selection)

description:
## Goal
Create a new SymbolSearchBar widget that replaces the hardcoded SymbolChipRow. It shows a search input with a dropdown overlay of matching Bybit pairs from marketScreenerItemsProvider.

## Effort Estimate
~3-4 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code:
   - lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/symbol_chip_row.dart - current hardcoded chips to replace
   - lib/presentation/providers/market_screener_provider.dart - marketScreenerItemsProvider has all pairs
   - lib/presentation/providers/selected_symbol_provider.dart - StateProvider to update on selection
   - lib/domain/entities/symbol_metadata.dart - chipLabel function for display

2. Write tests first (TDD):
   - test/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/symbol_search_bar_test.dart:
     - test_shows_search_input_field
     - test_typing_filters_screener_items_by_symbol (type BTC, see BTCUSDT)
     - test_typing_filters_case_insensitive (type btc, see BTCUSDT)
     - test_dropdown_shows_price_and_change_for_each_result
     - test_tapping_result_sets_selected_symbol_provider
     - test_dropdown_closes_on_selection
     - test_shows_loading_state_when_screener_data_loading
     - test_shows_currently_selected_symbol_as_label
     - test_empty_search_shows_top_results_limited_to_20

3. Implementation checklist:
   - [ ] Create lib/ui/widgets/trading_terminal/symbol_search_bar.dart
   - [ ] Widget is a ConsumerStatefulWidget with TextEditingController
   - [ ] Watches marketScreenerItemsProvider for the full symbol list
   - [ ] Filters items by search query (case-insensitive, matches rawSymbol and display symbol)
   - [ ] Shows Overlay with dropdown results
   - [ ] Each result row: symbol name, price formatted, 24h change percent colored
   - [ ] Tapping a result: sets selectedSymbolProvider, clears search, closes dropdown, dismisses keyboard
   - [ ] Shows currently selected symbol label when search is empty
   - [ ] Handles AsyncValue.loading and AsyncValue.error states
   - [ ] Limit dropdown to first 20 results for scroll performance
   - [ ] Replace SymbolChipRow usage in trading_terminal_screen.dart with SymbolSearchBar
   - [ ] Remove or deprecate symbol_chip_row.dart

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Search bar renders with input field
- [ ] Typing filters results by symbol name (case-insensitive)
- [ ] Dropdown shows price and 24h change for each result
- [ ] Tapping a result updates selectedSymbolProvider
- [ ] Dropdown closes after selection
- [ ] Loading state handled (spinner)
- [ ] Error state handled (retry or message)
- [ ] 9+ unit/widget tests pass
- [ ] flutter analyze clean

## Anti-Patterns
- NO hardcoded symbol lists (completeness: must work with any Bybit pair from screener)
- NO extra API calls for search (efficiency: filter existing screener data client-side)
- NO crashing when screener data is empty/loading (resilience: show appropriate states)
- NO showing unlimited results in dropdown (performance: cap at 20 visible results)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Screener Data Not Loaded**
- Terminal may be opened directly (not via Markets tab) — screener FutureProvider may not have fired yet.
- marketScreenerItemsProvider is a FutureProvider that auto-fetches on first watch. Watching it from SymbolSearchBar triggers the fetch.
- Show CircularProgressIndicator while loading, error message with retry if failed.

**Edge Case: Very Long Symbol Names**
- Some Bybit symbols can be long (e.g. 10000PEPEUSDT). Ensure dropdown text does not overflow.
- Use Expanded/Flexible in dropdown rows, ellipsis if needed.

**Edge Case: Empty Search Query**
- When search is empty, show the first 20 results sorted by volume.
- Do NOT show all 500+ pairs — cap at 20 for scroll performance.

**Edge Case: Special Characters in Search**
- Symbol names are alphanumeric. The search should handle any input gracefully.
- Simple contains() is sufficient — no SQL injection risk since it is client-side filtering.

**Edge Case: Keyboard Dismissal**
- After selecting a symbol, dismiss the keyboard via FocusScope.of(context).unfocus().
- After tapping outside the dropdown, close the dropdown and unfocus.

**Edge Case: Rapid Typing**
- User may type fast. Filtering is synchronous (in-memory list), so no debounce needed for ~500 items.
- If performance becomes an issue, add a 150ms debounce.

**Reference Implementation**
- Study Flutter Overlay API for dropdown implementation
- The dropdown should overlay on top of other terminal content
close_reason: SymbolSearchBar widget created with dropdown overlay, replaces hardcoded SymbolChipRow. All 729 tests pass, flutter analyze clean, dart format clean.

title: Fix _symbolFromRaw and resolveSymbolMetadata for dynamic symbol parsing

   - [ ] Fix _symbolFromRaw: for any symbol ending in USDT, strip USDT for base. For non-USDT, use raw as base. Always venue=Venue.bybit, quote=USDT.
   - [ ] Fix resolveSymbolMetadata: derive name from base (e.g. DOGE -> Dogecoin or just DOGE), decimals from price magnitude, class from asset class (perp/spot). Remove hardcoded switch or keep as override for known symbols with nice names.
   - [ ] Keep hardcoded entries for symbols with special names (BTC -> Bitcoin Perpetual) as overrides, but fall through to dynamic for unknown.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] resolveSymbolMetadata returns sensible metadata for any symbol
- [ ] Navigating from Markets with any symbol works
